Pak Study MCQs · pages 251-300

Sharpen your Pak Study recall

Tap a card to reveal the explanation. Take it one seal at a time.

500 questions in this set
1

How many Verses are in Surah “Al Fatiha”?

Correct Answer: 7
Show explanation
Explanation

surah Al Fatiah Other names: The Key, The Opener

surah Al Fatiah No. of verses: 7

surah Al Fatiah No. of words: 29

surah Al Fatiah No. of letters: 139
2

In which battle was Abdullah Bin Abi Bakr R.A with his soldiers martyred?

Correct Answer: Ghazwa e Uhad
Show explanation
Explanation
  • In the Battle of Uhud, 70 Muslims were martyred.
  • The Battle of Uhud occurred on Saturday, 23 March 625 AD (3 Hijri) in the valley north of Mount Uhud.
    • Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) led around 1000 Muslims in the Battle of Uhud.
    • They faced 3000 Qurayshi Meccans commanded by Abu Sufyan ibn Harb.
    • Abdullah bin Jubair was appointed by Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) to lead the 50 skilled archers positioned on Uhud Mountain.
    3

    Maseeh ul Islam is the title of _____?

    Correct Answer: Hazrat Abu Zar Ghaffari R.A
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Abu Dharr Al-Ghifari was the fourth or fifth person converting to Islam and from the Muhajirun.

    Died: 652 AD, Al Rabatha

    Full Name: Abu Dharr al-Ghifari al-Kinani

    4

    When Hazrat Ume Kalsoom RA died?

    Correct Answer: 630
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Born Hazrat Ume Kalsoom RA bint Muhammad 603 CE Mecca, Hejaz, Arabia

    Died: November, 630 CE.

    5

    Muslim thinker's category economy as ______?

    Correct Answer: Islamic economy
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Muslim thinkers categorize the economy as "Islamic economy," which is based on Islamic principles and laws.

    This framework emphasizes ethical and moral considerations in economic activities, including the prohibition of interest (riba) and promoting social justice.

    6

    Who is called Umar-e-Sani?

    Correct Answer: Hazrat Umar bin Abdul Azeez R.A
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Hazrat Umar bin Abdul Azeez R.A is known as "Umar-e-Sani" (the second Umar).

    Due to his just and pious rule, similar to that of Hazrat Umar bin Khattab R.A.

    He was the eighth Umayyad caliph.

    He is admired for his reforms and emphasis on justice.

    7

    OIC is related to _____?

    Correct Answer: Muslim World
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    OIC Stands for Organization of Islamic Cooperation
    OIC was Founded on September 25, 1969

    25 founding members of OIC at the time of its establishment.

    OIC headquarters is located in Jadda.

    It is the largest international organization outside of the United Nations.

    The official languages of the OIC are Arabic, English, and French.

    It changed its name on 28 June 2011 from the Organization of the Islamic Conference to its current name.

    Total Current member= 57

    Jakarta is the largest city in the Organisation of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) member countries

    8

    The Miracle of the camel was related to which Prophet?

    Correct Answer: Hazrat Saleh
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The Miracle of the Camel is a well-known story in Islamic tradition, associated with Prophet Saleh (Hazrat Saleh).

    According to the story, Prophet Saleh was sent to the tribe of Thamud, who were known for their wrongdoing.

    As a sign of his prophethood, Allah commanded Saleh to ask the tribe to slaughter a pregnant camel, which they refused to do.

    In response, Allah saved Saleh and his followers, while the tribe of Thamud was punished.

    9

    کس غزوہ کے بعد آپ صلی اللہ علیہ وآلہ وسلم نے الاسد نام کے گھر میں تین دن قیام کیا؟

    Correct Answer: غزوہ احد
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    حضور ﷺ نے غزوہ احد کے بعد تین دن تک "حمراۃ الاسد" میں قیام فرمایا۔

    یہ قیام مشرکین کو دوبارہ مدینہ پر حملہ کرنے سے روکنے کے لیے تھا۔ 


    10

    ام حبیبہ کس کی بیٹی تھی؟

    Correct Answer: ابو سفیان
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Umm-ul-Momineen Bibi Umm-e-Habiba

    Wife of

    The Holy Prophet (PBUH)

    Real Name

    Ramla

    Daughter of

    Abu Sufyan

    Umm-ul-Momineen

    Mother of believers

    11

    من و سلوی کس نبی کی امت پر نازل ہوا؟

    Correct Answer: حضرت موسی
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    من و سلویٰ ایک قسم کی خوراک تھا، جس کا ذکر قرآن مجید کی سورہ البقرہ میں ہوا ہے

    12

    الله سبحان و تعالی کا ذاتی نام کون سا ہے؟

    Correct Answer: اللہ
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    اللّہ خدا کا مخصوص نام ہے جو تمام اوصاف کمالیہ رکھنے والی ذات کیلئے بولا جاتا ہے ۔

    13

    ایک سو چورانوے (194) سے دو سو چھپن (256) ہجری کس کا دور؟

    Correct Answer: امام بخاری
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Muḥammad ibn Ismā‘īl al-Bukhārī, commonly referred to as Imam al-Bukhari or Imam Bukhari, was a Persian Islamic scholar who was born in Bukhara. 

    Born: July 20, 810 AD,Uzbekistan

    Died: September 1, 870 AD, Uzbekistan

    14

    Who put circles for the first time in Quran-e-Pak?

    Correct Answer: Hazart Abu al-Aswad al-Du’ali RA
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Abu al-Aswad devised a system of dots to signal the three short vowels (along with their respective allophones) of Arabic.

    15

    When Cyprus was conquered?

    Correct Answer: 25 Hijri
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Cyprus was conquered by the Ottoman Empire in 1571.

    This conquest was part of the larger Ottoman–Venetian Wars

    The island remained officially part of the Ottoman Empire until it was formally annexed by Britain in 1914

    16

    What is the meaning of Etikaf?

    Correct Answer: Seclusion
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Seclusion            تنہائی

    During the last 10 days of Ramadan (Etikaf), many Muslims spend their days and nights at the mosque in order to ensure that they are there for whichever night Laylat al-Qadr occurs.
    17

    What was the first written constitution of the world?

    Correct Answer: Meesaq-e-Madina
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Misaq e Madinawas the first written constitution of the world

    ****

    The Constitution of Medina, written on behalf of the Islamic prophet Muhammad in 622 CE

     written agreement (Meesaq e Madina) was signed between the Muslims and Jews.

    In Misaq e Madina total articles are 47

    The first Islamic State was established by Muhammad in Medina under the Constitution of Medina.

    ******

    The oldest written constitution of government in effect in the USA

    ****

    ND9-11-2022

    18

    Mustadrak Hakim written was by _____?

    Correct Answer: Hakim al-Nishapuri
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Al-Mustadrak 'ala al-Sahihayn is a five volume hadith collection written by Hakim al-Nishapuri (Nishapur is located in Iran).


    19

    Who was the student of Hazrat Abu Huraira who helped him to compile Ahadith?

    Correct Answer: Hamman Ibn Munabbah
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Sahifah Hammam ibn Munabbih : the earliest extant work on the Hadith ; comprising as-Sahifah as-Sahihah of Abu-Hurairah prepared for his pupil Hammam Ibn Munabbih.


    20

    What was the real name of Abu Jehal?

    Correct Answer: Umer bin Hisham
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Umer ibn Hishām al-Makhzumi, was one of the Meccan polytheist pagan Qurayshi leaders.
    Abu Jehal Born:     572 AD, Mecca, Saudi Arabia
    Abu Jehal Died:     March 17, 624 AD, Badr, Saudi Arabia
    21

    In Hazrat Abu Bakr's era the siege of Damascus by Muslim Army was started on ______?

    Correct Answer: 21 August 634 CE
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The siege of Damascus (634) lasted from 21 August to 19 September 634 before the city fell to the Rashidun Caliphate.


    22

    What was the Surname of mother of Hazrat Abu Bakar (R.A)?

    Correct Answer: Ummul Khair
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The mother of Hazrat Abu Bakr (R.A.) was Ummul Khair, whose real name was Salma bint Sakhar.

    She was a noble woman from the Tribe of Banu Taym in Mecca.

    Hazrat Abu Bakr (R.A.) was the first Caliph of Islam.

    23

    The meaning of Khawarij is ____?

    Correct Answer: Rebels
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The Khawarij sect emerged as a result of the Battle of Siffin (657 CE).

    It was fought between the forces of Caliph Hazrat Ali and those of Muawiyah ibn Abu Sufyan. 

    The meaning of Khawarij is "rebels."

    The battle ended in a stalemate, and the arbitration process that followed led to a group of rebels, who would later become known as the Khawarij, to break away from Hazrat Ali's camp.

    24

    Writer of Al Kafi Muhammad bin Yaqub Al Kuliyani died in _____?

    Correct Answer: 329 Hijri
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    He travelled to Baghdad for this reason and lived there for twenty years, engaged in teaching and pursuing academic work, until he died in 329 AH/941 CE.

    He is considered the foremost Shia compiler of hadith and was the author of Kitab al-Kafi.


    25

    How many wives were alive at the time of the Prophet Muhammad's death?

    Correct Answer: 9
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Mother of the Believers is a term by which each of Muhammad's wives came to be prefixed over time.

    It is derived from Quran 33:6:

    26

    Ethics deals with “Ethical Action” is termed as _____?

    Correct Answer: Normative Ethics
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Ethics dealing with “Ethical Action” is termed as Normative Ethics.

    It focuses on setting standards for right and wrong conduct and provides guidelines on how individuals should act.

    27

    قرآن کریم میں نفس کی کتنی اقسام بیان کی گئی ہیں؟

    Correct Answer: 3
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    نفس مطئمہ

    نفس لواما

    نفس امارہ

    28

    نبی کریم صلی اللّٰہ تعالیٰ علیہ وآلہ وسلم کا نام احمد قرآن کریم کی کس سورہ میں آیا ہے؟

    Correct Answer: سورہ صف
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    کلام پاک میں نام احمد ایک مرتبہ جبکہ نام محمد چار مرتبہ آیا ہے

    29

    There are _____ Jamarats.

    Correct Answer: 3
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The Jamarats are three walls located in Mina, Saudi Arabia, that are part of the Hajj pilgrimage.

    Pilgrims throw 7 stones at these walls to symbolize the rejection of sin and temptation.


    There are three Jamarats:

    1. Jamrat al-Aqaba (the largest pillar)
    2. Jamrat al-Wusta (the middle pillar)
    3. Jamrat al-Ula (the smallest pillar)
    30

    Raheel was the wife of which Prophet?

    Correct Answer: Hazrat Yaqoob (A.S.)
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Raheel(AS) was the wife of Yuqub(AS) and the mother of Yusuf(AS)

    31

    When Khalid Bin Waleed was awarded the title of "Saif-Ullah"?

    Correct Answer: None of these
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    It was only after the Battle of Mutah (629 A.D.)

    —wherein Khalid broke nine swords during the fight

    —that he was awarded the title of "Saif Ullah al-Maslool" (The Drawn Sword of Allah)

    He was a Muslim commander in the service of the:

    • Prophet Muhammad
    • Caliph Abu Bakr
    • Umar

    He was  born in  592 AD, in Mecca

    He Died: in 642 AD, in Homs‎, Syria

    They were Buried in Khaled Ibn Walid Mosque, in Syria

    32

    In which religion Angel Mikaeel is preferred on Angel Jibreel?

    Correct Answer: Jewism
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Angel Jibril (Gabriel) is referred to as "Rooh ul Ameen" 

    Hazrat Jibrail عَلَيْهِ ٱلسَّلَامُ is the Angel of Allah Almighty who used to bring down revelations to the prophet

    The Holy Qurn mentions Hazrat Jibreel (AS) by name only three times.

    He is the chief angel.

    33

    I have sent only for the purpose of perfecting good morals is the saying of

    Correct Answer: Hazrat Muhammad (PBUH)
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The saying "I have been sent only for the purpose of perfecting good morals" (Arabic: إِنَّمَا بُعِثْتُ لِأُتَمِّمَ مَكَارِمَ الْأَخْلَاقِ) 

    It is a well-known hadith (saying) of the Prophet Muhammad (PBUH)

    34

    The holy Prophet Hazrat Muhammad (PBUH) set up Dar-e-Arqam in?

    Correct Answer: Madina
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Dar-e-Arqam” was established at Hazrat Arqam Bin Arqam's house in the plains of Koh-e-Safa by Hazrat Muhammad (S.A.W).

    After Hijrah (migration) the madrasa of "Suffa" was established in Madina.

    The first Dar-e-Arqam School was established in Sargodha in 1991

    35

    Which Prophet is called the Father of Muslims?

    Correct Answer: Hazrat Abraheem (A.S)
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Hazrat Ibrahim is called the Father of Muslim

    The Qur'an refers to him as the friend of God (4:125) and the father of prophets


    Additional Information

    Hazrat Ibrahim A.S remained on fire for 40 days

    Khalil Ullah is the title of Hazrat Ibrahim (A.S)

    Hazrat Ibrahim (A.S) was born  in Iraq

    Hazrat Ibrahim's (AH) name is mentioned in the Holy Quran 67 times.

    Abraham had two children,

    1.      Isaac

    2.      Ismael,


    RY30012023ND-21-5-2023

    36

    Who was the first Holy Prophet's children to be born?

    Correct Answer: Hazrat Qasim
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Hazrat Qasim was the first child of the Holy Prophet (PUBH)

    Three Sons of Prophet (PBUH)

    1. Hazrat Al-Qasim was born from HAZRAT KHADIJ
    2. Hazrat Abdullah was born from HAZRAT KHADIJ
    3. Hazrat Ibrahim was born from Hazrat Maria


    Additional Information

    There are Four Daughters of the Prophet (PBUH)
    1. Hadhrat Zainab (RA)
    2. Hadhrat Ruqayyah (RA)
    3. Hadhrat Umme Kulthum (RA)
    4. Hadhrat Faatimah (RA)
    Hazrat Muhammad PBUH Had 11th Wives.


    37

    Who suggested to translate Holy Quran in local languages in subcontinent firstly:

    Correct Answer: Shah Wali Ullah
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Shah Waliullah, for the first time in history, translated the Holy Quran into Persian Language

    Shah Wali Ullah was born in the reign of Aurangzeb,

    Shah Waliullah Dehlawi was Born in 21 February 1703 in Phulat, Mughal Empire

    Shah Waliullah Dehlawi Died in 20 August 1762 (aged 59) Dehli, Mughal Empire


    38

    What is Usher and Zakat in islam?

    Correct Answer: Two kinds of Tax in Islam
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Usher and Zakat are Two kinds of Tax.


    Zakat is a form of almsgiving treated in Islam as a tax and is one of the Five Pillars of Islam.

    Usher refers to a form of tax that is a tithe (one-tenth) on agricultural produce.

    39

    The name of Hazrat Bilal's (R.A) master was ______ ?

    Correct Answer: Umayyah bin Khailf
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Hazrat Bilal's (R.A) master was Umayyah bin Khailf

    Bilal was known for his hard work and loyalty to his master, Umayyah ibn Khalaf

    Umayya ibn Khalaf was an Arab slave master 

    ***

    Bilal's mother was abducted as a child and sold as a slave.

    He was also the first muezzin (prayer caller) in the Muslim faith.

    The Title of Bilal ibn Rabah: Sayyid al-Muaddin

    He was Born: 580 AD, Mecca

    He was Died: on March 2, 640 AD, in Damascus, Syria

    His Place of burial is Bab el Saghir Cemetery, Damascus, Syria


    40

    Which punishment was introduced by Hazrat Umar (RA)?

    Correct Answer: Imprisonment
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • During the caliphate of Hazrat Umar (RA), imprisonment was introduced as a punishment for certain crimes.
    • Prior to this, punishments were largely limited to fines, lashes, or death.
    41

    The Shortest Surah Al-Kawthar has ______ verses?

    Correct Answer: 3
    Show explanation
    Explanation
     The Surah Al Kawthar is the shortest surah of Holy Quran

    (3 Verses).


    The Surah Baqarah is longest     surah of Holy Quran

    (286 Verses).

    42

    "There is no compulsion in religion" contained in which of the following Surah?

    Correct Answer: Al-Baqarah
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The phrase "There is no compulsion in religion" is contained in Surah Al-Baqarah, Ayat 256.

    The full quote is:

    "There is no compulsion in religion, the right direction is henceforth distinct from error."

    (Surah Al-Baqarah, 2:256)

    This verse emphasizes the importance of freedom of choice in matters of faith and religion.

    43

    Which department got functional during Hazrat Omer Bin Abdul Aziz period?

    Correct Answer: Bait-ul-Mal
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Bait-ul-Mal, or the Department of Public Treasury, was established during the caliphate of Hazrat Umar bin Abdul Aziz (717-720 CE).

    This department was responsible for managing the state's finances, collecting taxes, and distributing wealth among the citizens.

    44

    Where did Hazrat Muhammad (PBUH) and Hazrat Abu Bakar (R.A) stay during migration to Madinah?

    Correct Answer: Cave of Soor
    Show explanation
    Explanation

     Holy Prophet (PBUH) and Hazrat Abu Bakar (R.A) took shelter in the Cave of Soor.

    They found shelter for three days in Ghar e Soor 

    They took refuge from the Quraysh

    Allah (SWT) miraculously had a spider weave a web at the cave’s entrance on the third day of their stay

    Ghar e Soor also known as Jabal al-thawr ( Cave of Thawr, Mount Thawr)

    Located in the lower part of Makkah, Saudi Arabia

    **

    About ABU BAKAR

    Abu Bakr was a sahabi, a companion of Muhammad

    He was the first Caliph of Islam.

    He was also Muhammad's father-in-law through Aisha.

    His real name was Abdullah or Abul-Kaaba and Abu Bakr was his kunya.

    Hazrat Abu Bakar collected the Quran in the form of a book

    45

    Name the Masjid (Mosque) in which Holy prophet Muhammad (SAW) led the prayer during Miraaj?

    Correct Answer: Masjid-e-Aqsa
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    During the event of Miraaj, the Holy Prophet Muhammad (SAW) led the prayer at Masjid-e-Aqsa, which is located in Jerusalem.


    The Miraj event took place on the 27th of Rajab in the 10th (621 A.D) year of Prophethood.

    Prophet Muhammad (SAW) was staying at the house of Umm Hani on the night of Miraj.

    That night, Angel Jibreel came to the Prophet (SAW) and took him on the journey of Israa and Miraj.

    During Miraj, Hazrat Adam and Hazrat Muhammad (SAW) met at the first heaven.

    The event is mentioned in Surah Bani Israel.

    It is also known as 'Isra and Mi'raj' or 'Shab-e-Meraj'. 

    • Isra: Night journey from Makkah to Masjid Al-Aqsa.
    • Mi’raj: Journey from Masjid Al-Aqsa to the heavens.

    Gifts of the 27th of Rajab:

    • The five prescribed daily prayers.
    • The last part of the chapter of Al-Baqara (Amanar-Rasulu).
    46

    Which Surah of the Holy Quran confirms the Islamic Religion as the complete Religion?

    Correct Answer: Al-Maidah
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Surah Al-Maidah (Chapter 5) of the Holy Quran confirms Islam as the complete and final religion, as stated in Verse 3:

    •  "This day, I have perfected your religion for you, completed My favor upon you, and have chosen for you Islam as your religion." (Quran 5:3)

    This verse emphasizes that Islam is the final and complete religion, superseding all previous religions and completing the message of Allah.

    47

    قواعد التحدیث کے مصنف کون ہیں ؟

    Correct Answer: محمد جمال الدین قاسمی
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    قواعد التحدیث کے مصنف محمد جمال الدین قاسمی ہیں۔






    48

    الاتقان فی علوم القرآن کے مصنف ہیں ؟

    Correct Answer: جلال الدین سیوطی
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    یہ کتاب علوم القرآن کے موضوع پر ایک مشہور اور مستند تصنیف ہے، جس میں قرآن کی مختلف علوم و معارف کا تفصیلی جائزہ لیا گیا ہے۔

    49

    حضور صلی اللہ علم کی لحد کسی صحابی نے تیار کی ؟

    Correct Answer: حضرت ابو طلحہ انصاری
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    حضور صلی اللہ علیہ وسلم کی لحد حضرت ابو طلحہ انصاری رضی اللہ عنہ نے تیار کی تھی۔


    50

    قِيْلَ ارْجِعُوا وَرَاءَكُمْ فَالْتَمِسُوا نُورًا اس آیت میں کس گروه کی طرف اشارہ ہے؟

    Correct Answer: منافقین
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    یہ آیت سورۃ الحدید کی ہے اور قیامت کے دن منافقین کا حال بیان کرتی ہے

    جب ان کو کہا جائے گا کہ وہ پیچھے جائیں اور نور تلاش کریں، کیونکہ ان کے پاس ایمان کا نور نہیں ہوگا

    51

    امام الفقہاء کن کا لقب ہے ؟

    Correct Answer: حضرت معاذ بن جبل
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    امام الفقہاء کا لقب حضرت معاذ بن جبل رضی اللہ عنہ کا ہے۔


    حضرت معاذ بن جبل رضی اللہ عنہ کو ان کی فقہی بصیرت اور علم کی بنیاد پر یہ لقب دیا گیا تھا۔ وہ رسول اللہ ﷺ کے قریب ترین صحابہ میں شمار ہوتے تھے اور ان سے فقہ کے مسائل میں رہنمائی لی جاتی تھی۔

    52

    خلق القرآن کن کا عقیدہ ہے ؟

    Correct Answer: معتزلہ
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    خلق القرآن کا عقیدہ  معتزلہ کا ہے


    معتزلہ کا یہ عقیدہ تھا کہ قرآن کریم مخلوق ہے، جبکہ اہل سنت والجماعت کا عقیدہ ہے کہ قرآن غیر مخلوق ہے اور اللہ کی صفات میں سے ایک صفت ہے۔

    53

    آیت تخییر کا ذکر کس سورت میں ہے ؟

    Correct Answer: سورة المائدة
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    آیت تخییر کا ذکر سورة المائدة سورت میں ہے



    آیت تخییر، جو کہ ازواج مطہرات کو اپنے حقوق کے بارے میں اختیار دینے کا ذکر کرتی ہے، سورة المائدة میں موجود ہے۔

    54

    محصنات کن لوگوں کو کہا جاتا ہے ؟

    Correct Answer: پاکیزہ عورتیں
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    محصنات کن لوگوں کو کہا جاتا ہے: پاکیزہ عورتیں


    محصنات کا لفظ عام طور پر پاکیزہ، باعصمت اور شریف عورتوں کے لیے استعمال ہوتا ہے۔

    55

    اسماءالرجال کےفن کا تعلق ہے؟

    Correct Answer: حدیث سے
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    اسماء الرجال ایک فن ہے جو راویوں کی شناخت، ان کی خصوصیات، اور ان کی روایات کی صحت پر غور کرتا ہے۔ یہ علم حدیث کے ضمن میں آتا ہے۔

    56

    تفسیر طبری کتنے عرصے میں مکمل ہوئی؟

    Correct Answer: سات سال
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    تفسیر طبری مکمل ہونے میں : سات سال لگے


    امام طبری نے یہ تفسیر عربی میں لکھنے کا آغاز 839 عیسوی میں کیا اور 846 عیسوی میں مکمل کی

    57

    تاج العروس کس موضوع پر ہے ؟

    Correct Answer: لغت
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    تاج العروس کا موضوع ہے:لغت

    یہ کتاب عربی لغت کی ایک مشہور اور مستند کتاب ہے، جو "قاموس المحیط" کی شرح ہے۔یہ ایک مشہور عربی لغت کی کتاب ہے جسے مرتضی زبیدی نے لکھا ہے، اور یہ لسان العرب کی تشریح اور توضیح پر مبنی ہے۔


     "تاج العروس" مرتضی زبیدی کی تصنیف ہے۔


    "تاج العروس" کی تصنیف 18ویں صدی میں مکمل ہوئی تھی۔

    مرتضی زبیدی نے اس کتاب کو 1760 میں مکمل کیا تھا۔


    58

    قرآن پاک کی مکمل سائنسی تفسیر ہے ؟

    Correct Answer: تفسیر الجواهر از علامه طنطاوی جوهری
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    قرآن پاک کی مکمل سائنسی تفسیر ہے:تفسیر الجواهر از علامہ طنطاوی جوہری


    یہ تفسیر خاص طور پر قرآن کی آیات کو سائنسی نقطہ نظر سے بیان کرنے کی ایک کوشش ہے،

    اور علامہ طنطاوی جوہری نے قرآن کی تفسیر کو سائنسی حقائق کے ساتھ جوڑنے کی کوشش کی ہے۔

    59

    حضرت امام ابن منبہ نے الصحیفہ الصحیحہ میں کس کی احادیث جمع کی ہیں ؟

    Correct Answer: حضرت ابو ہریرہ رضی اللہ عنہ
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    حضرت ابو ہریرہ رضی اللہ عنہ سے روایت کردہ احادیث کو الصحیفہ الصحیحہ میں جمع کیا گیا ہے۔

    60

    حد قذف کا ذکر کس سورہ میں ہے ؟

    Correct Answer: سوره نور
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    حد قذف کا ذکر ہے: سوره نور


    سورہ نور کی آیت 4 میں حد قذف (جھوٹا الزام لگانے) کے بارے میں واضح حکم دیا گیا ہے۔

    61

    لِلْفُقَرَآءِ الْمُهٰجِرِیْنَ الَّذِیْنَ اُخْرِجُوْا مِنْ دِیَارِهِمْ سے کون سی نص مراد ہے؟

    Correct Answer: عبارة النص
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    لِلْفُقَرَآءِ الْمُهٰجِرِیْنَ الَّذِیْنَ اُخْرِجُوْا مِنْ دِیَارِهِمْ سے مراد ہے:عبارة النص


    کیونکہ یہ آیت واضح اور صریح الفاظ میں مہاجرین فقہاء کا ذکر کر رہی ہے جنہیں ان کے گھروں سے نکالا گیا تھا۔ عبارت النص وہ ہوتی ہے جس میں کوئی حکم یا بات براہ راست اور صریح انداز میں بیان کی گئی ہو، جیسے اس آیت میں فقیر مہاجرین کا ذکر کیا گیا ہے۔

    62

    وَ أُبْرِئُ الْأَكْمَةِ وَ الْأَبْرَصَ میں کس پیغمبر کے معجزات ہیں ؟

    Correct Answer: حضرت عیسی
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    یہ معجزات حضرت عیسیٰ علیہ السلام کے ہیں، جن میں وہ اندھے (اکمہ) اور برص کے مریض (ابرص) کو صحت عطا کرتے تھے

    63

    زاد المعاد في هدی خیر العباد کا موضوع کیا ہے ؟

    Correct Answer: سیرت
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    اد المعاد في هدی خیر العباد کا موضوع  سیرت ہے


    یہ کتاب امام ابن قیم الجوزیہ کی لکھی ہوئی ہے اور اس میں نبی اکرم صلی اللہ علیہ وآلہ وسلم کی سیرت، تعلیمات، اور اخلاقی خصوصیات کا تفصیلی بیان کیا گیا ہے۔

    64

    دا علمهم بالحلال والحرام کون سے صحابی مراد ہیں؟

    Correct Answer: حضرت معاذ بن جبل
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    یہ حدیث حضرت معاذ بن جبل رضی اللہ عنہ کے بارے میں ہے، جو علمِ حلال و حرام میں ماہر تھے اور رسول اللہ صلی اللہ علیہ وآلہ وسلم نے انہیں علم سکھایا۔







    65

    How many times the word ‘Qul’ has been repeated in the Holy Quran?

    Correct Answer: More than 200
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The word "Qul" (قُلْ) is an Arabic word that means "Say" or "Proclaim", and it is a command to the Prophet Muhammad (SAW) to convey the message of Allah.

    It has been repeated 332 times in the Holy Quran.


    There are 5 Surah in Holy Quran which start with the word Qul:

    1.      Al-Jinn(72),

    2.      Al-Kafiroon(109),

    3.      Al-Ikhlas(112),

    4.      Al-Falaq(113),

    5.      Al-Naas(114)

    66

    Who among the following was the first Asian translate the Holy Quran into English?

    Correct Answer: Abdullah Yousaf Ali
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Abdullah Yusuf Ali (1872-1953) was a British-Indian Islamic scholar and translation.

    He was best known for his English translation of the Quran, "The Meaning of the Holy Quran".

    Published in 1934, it was the first English translation of the Quran by an Asian scholar.

    67

    نبی کریم ملی ایم نے دس سال عہد میں کتنا علاقہ فتح کیا ؟

    Correct Answer: و سلاکھ مربع میل
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    نبی کریم ﷺ کے دس سالہ دورِ مدنیہ میں تقریباً سوا لاکھ مربع میل علاقہ اسلام کے زیرِ نگین آیا تھا۔

    اس عرصے میں اسلامی ریاست کا دائرہ مکہ، مدینہ، خیبر، طائف، اور دیگر قریبی علاقوں تک پھیل چکا تھا۔

    اس کے ساتھ ہی ارد گرد کے علاقوں میں بھی اسلام کا پیغام پہنچا اور کئی قبائل نے اسلام قبول کیا۔

    یہ فتوحات اور پھیلاؤ اس حکمتِ عملی اور عادلانہ طرزِ حکمرانی کی بنیاد پر ہوا جو نبی کریم ﷺ نے اپنایا تھا،

    جس میں دینِ اسلام کا فروغ اور اخلاقی تعلیمات کا نفاذ شامل تھا۔

    68

    احکام عشرہ کا ذکر کون سی سورۃ میں موجود ہے؟

    Correct Answer: سورة انعام
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    سورة الانعام - آیت 152

    موسیٰ علیہ السلام کو کوہ طور پر لکھی لکھائی تختیاں دی گئیں۔
    ان میں دس احکام مذکور تھے جنہیں احکام عشرہ کہتے ہیں
    ۔ یہ احکام بعد میں تورات میں شامل کردیئے گئے۔
    ان دس احکام میں سے ایک حکم سبت کے دن کی تعظیم تھا۔
    اگر اسے نکال دیا جائے تو باقی یہی نو احکام رہ جاتے ہیں جو ان آیات میں مذکور ہیں
    69

    کسی مسجد کی بنیاد تقوی پر رکھی گئی ہے ؟

    Correct Answer: مسجد قباء
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    مسجد قباء مسلمانوں کے دلوں میں بڑا مقام رکھتی ہے:

    ارشاد باری تعالیٰ ہے: ''جس مسجد کی بنیاد پہلے دن سے تقویٰ پر رکھی گئی ہ ہو زیادہ حقدار ہے کہ آپ اس میں کھڑے ہوں اس میں وہ لوگ ہیں جو پاکیزگی کو پسند کرتے ہیں، اور اللہ تعالیٰ پاکیزگی والوں کو پسند کرتا ہے۔''

    70

    What was the cause of death of Hazrat Usman (RA)?

    Correct Answer: Martyred by rebels
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Hazrat Usman (R.A)

    Belonged

    Quraysh

    Born

    Makkah

    Caliph

    Third caliph

    Caliphate

    23 to 35 Hijjah

    Caliphate

    644 to 656 CE

    Titles

    Zun-Noorain & Jame-al-Quran

    Martyred

    18th Zul-Hijjah 35 A.H

    Martyrdom day

    Friday

    Wives

    Ruqayyah (RA) & Umm-e-Kulthum (RA)

    Full Name

    Usman bin Affaan

    Death cause

    Martyred by rebels

    Know everything about Hazrat Usman (R.A)

    Hazrat Usman (RA) at the persuasion of Hazrat Abu-Bakr (RA) accepted Islam.

    Wives of Hazrat Usman (RA) namely Ruqayyah (RA) & Umm-e-Kulthum (RA) were the daughter of the Holy Prophet (PBUH).

    Hazrat Usman (RA) could not participate in Battle of Badr because his wife Ruqayyah (RA) was on deathbed.

    After the death of Ruqayyah (RA), Hazrat Usman (RA) married with Umm-e-Kulthum (RA).

    Ruqayyah (RA died on victory day of Battle of Badr.

    Hazrat Usman (RA) purchased the adjoining land for 25,000 dirham to extend Masjid-e-Nabavi.

    Hazrat Usman used to enfranchise a slave on every Friday.

    Muslim Navy was also developed during the period of Hazrat Usman (RA).

     Zun-Noorain means Possessor of Two Lights

    Jame-al-Quran means compiler of Quran. 


    71

    A person who is liable to pay Zakat is called __________?

    Correct Answer: Sahib-e-Nisab
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The nisab by the gold standard is 3 ounces of gold (87.48 grammes) or its equivalent in cash.

    The nisab by the silver standard is 21 ounces of silver (612.36 grammes) or its equivalent in cash.

    72

    Who was the sixth caliph of the Umayyad dynasty during the Debal incident?

    Correct Answer: Walid bin Abdul Malik
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Walid bin Abdul Malik was the sixth caliph of the Umayyad dynasty, ruling from 705 to 715 CE.

    During his reign, the Debal incident occurred in 711 CE, when the Arab governor of Sindh, Muhammad bin Qasim, conquered the port city of Debal (now Karachi) in modern-day Pakistan.

    73

    Who interpreted neo-Sufi concept of Islam?

    Correct Answer: Shah Wali-Ullah
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Shah Wali-Ullah (1703-1762) was a prominent Islamic scholar.

    He was a reformer who interpreted the neo-Sufi concept of Islam.

    He was a member of the Naqshbandi Sufi order.

    Shah Wali-Ullah emphasized the importance of inner spiritual growth, purification of the heart, and the pursuit of knowledge.

    74

    Who among the following is not in Ashara-e-Mubashara?

    Correct Answer: Hazrat Abu Huraira (RA)
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Ashra Mubashra are those Companions of Prophet Hazrat Muhammad (P.B.U.H), who were given Good News in their life that they will be awarded Heaven.


    Following is the List with the Names of Ashra Mubashra.


    1. Hazrat Abu Bakar (R.A)


    2. Hazrat Umer Farooq (R.A)


    3. Hazrat Usman (R.A)


    4. Hazrat Ali (R.A)


    5. Hazrat Zubair ibn-e-Awam (R.A)


    6. Hazrat Talha (R.A)


    7. Hazrat Abdul Rehman bin-e-Auf (R.A)


    8. Hazrat Abu Obaida bin-al-Jarah (R.A)


    9. Hazrat Saad bin-e-Abi Waqas (R.A)


    10. Hazrat Saeed bin-e-Zaid (R.A)

    SH1-8-2023

    75

    The Paradise has ________ doors.

    Correct Answer: 8
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Paradise (Jannah) is described as having eight doors in Islamic teachings.

    Each door represents different acts of worship, such as prayer (Salat), charity (Zakat), fasting (Sawm), Hajj, jihad, and others.

    76

    The name of the mosque established by “Munafiqeen” in Madina was _____?

    Correct Answer: Zarar
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The mosque established by the "Munafiqeen" (hypocrites) in Madina was called Masjid al-Zarar.

    It was built with malicious intentions, and Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) ordered its destruction in 9 Hijri after its true purpose was revealed.

    77

    The Prophet (SW) remained in Mecca after Nubuwat for _______ years.

    Correct Answer: 13
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) remained in Mecca for 13 years after receiving prophethood (Nubuwat).

    During this period, he preached Islam before migrating to Medina in 622 CE (Hijrah).

    78

    Ghazwa Tabook is fought against _____?

    Correct Answer: Romans
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The Battle of Tabuk is known as the last Gazwa of the Holy Prophet

    Ghazwa Tabook is fought against: Romans

    Ghazwa e Tabook was fought in the year: 9 Hijri

    initiated by Muhammad in October 630 AD.

    It took place During Rajab and Sha'ban in the region of Tabuk

    20 days Hazrat Muhammad PBUH stayed during Tabook Battle

    ***

    ND29-1-2023



    79

    Whose son was Hazrat Ikram R.A?

    Correct Answer: Abu Jehal
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Hazrat Ikrimah R.A was the son of Abu Jehal.

    He was a prominent leader of the Quraysh tribe who opposed Prophet Muhammad (PBUH).

    After initially opposing Islam, Ikrimah later embraced the faith and became a companion of the Prophet (PBUH).

    80

    In which battle did 70 Muslims embrace martyrdom?

    Correct Answer: Uhad
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • In the Battle of Uhud, 70 Muslims were martyred.
    • The Battle of Uhud occurred on Saturday, 23 March 625 AD (3 Hijri) in the valley north of Mount Uhud.
      • Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) led around 1000 Muslims in the Battle of Uhud.
      • They faced 3000 Qurayshi Meccans commanded by Abu Sufyan ibn Harb.
      • Abdullah bin Jubair was appointed by Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) to lead the 50 skilled archers positioned on Uhud Mountain.
      81

      Which surah of the Holy Quran is named after Honey Bee?

      Correct Answer: An-Nahl
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      There are more than 200 verses in the Quran dealing with animals

      The biggest animal mentioned in Holy Quran Elephant.
      The smallest animal mentioned in Holy Quran       Mosquito.

      The six chapters of the Quran are named after animals or insects:

      1.      Surat 2, Al Baqarah (The Cow);

      2.      Surat 6, Al Anaam (The Cattle),

      3.      Surat 16, Al Nahl (The Bees);

      4.      Surat 27, Al Naml (The Ants);

      5.      Surat 29, Al Ankabut (The Spider);

      6.      Surat 105, Al Fil.

      82

      What was the age of Rasool Ullah (PBUH) at the time of first Wahi?

      Correct Answer: 40 years 6 months
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      پہلی وحی کے وقت رسول اللہ صلی اللہ علیہ وسلم کی عمر کتنی تھی؟

      40 years 6 months

      When the first wahi was revealed to the Holy Prophet ﷺ?

      610AD

      ****


      What was the age of Rasool Ullah (PBUH) at the time of the first Wahi?

      40 years 6 months

      ********



      How many verses (Ayats) were in the first Wahi?

      5

      ********


      Name the first prescriber of wahi in Makkah.

      Sharjeel-ibn-Hasana Kandi

      ***

      Who was the first writer of “Wahi” in Quraish?
      Hazrat Zaid bin Sabit (RA)

      ********

      The first Wahi was descended on Cave Hira.

      پہلی وحی غارحرا میں نازل ہوئی تھی۔

      ****

      ND10-1-2023

      ***

      ND28-1-2023


      83

      To which clan of Quraish Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) belong?

      Correct Answer: Bannu Hashim
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Family of the Prophet Hazrat Muhammad ﷺ was Hashmi and the Tribe was Quraish.

      ***

      Holy Prophet Hazrat Muhammad ﷺ great-grandfather was Hashim ibn Abd Manaf, for whom the tribe is named.

      Members of this clan are referred to as Hashmi.

      Banu Hashim is a clan in the Quraysh tribe.

      **** 

      RY18012023


      84

      Hazrat Ibrahim A.S remained in fire ___ days.

      Correct Answer: 40 days
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      important question : 

      Namrud ordered throwing Hazrat Ibrahim (A.S) into the fire

      Nimrod, the king of Babylon, commanded his men to throw Prophet Ibrahim (AS) into a huge pit of burning fire

      Hazrat Ibrahim A.S remained on fire for 40 days

      Khalil Ullah is the title of Hazrat Ibrahim (A.S)

      Hazrat Ibrahim (A.S) was born Iraq

      Hazrat Ibrahim's (AH) name is mentioned in the Holy Quran 67 times.

      Abraham had two children,

      1.      Isaac

      2.      Ismael,

      ****

      agm.04.03.23


      ND5-2-2023

      85

      He had knowledge of the interpretation of dreams?

      Correct Answer: Hazrat Yousuf (AS)
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      •  Hazrat Yousuf (AS) had knowledge of the interpretation of dreams.
      • Known as "The Truthful One" (/siddiq/) in the Quran for his accurate dream interpretations.
      • The other is Abu Bakr, who knew dreams.
      86

      Which Umayyad caliph initiated the conquest of Spain?

      Correct Answer: Tariq Ibn Ziyad
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      In 711 Muslim forces invaded and in seven years conquered the Iberian peninsula.

      It became one of the great Muslim civilizations; reaching its summit with the Umayyad caliphate of Cordovain the tenth century. Muslim rule declined after that and ended in 1492 when Granada was conquered.

      87

      What was the 1st civil war in Islam?

      Correct Answer: Jamal
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Battle of the Camel, also known as the Battle of Jamel or the Battle of Basra,

      Battle of Jamel took place outside of Basra, Iraq, in 36 AH/656 CE.

      The battle was fought between the army of the fourth caliph, Hazrat Ali رضہ, on one side, and the other army led by Hazrat Aisha رضہ,

      88

      Usher and Khums are _____?

      Correct Answer: Two kinds of Tax in Islam
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Usher and Khums are Two kinds of Tax in Islam,

      In Islam, KHUMS خمس refers to the required religious obligation of any Muslims to pay one-fifth of their acquired wealth from certain sources toward specified causes. Khums is different and separate from other Islamic taxes such as zakat and jizya.

      89

      Backbiting is like eating flesh of one's dead _______?

      Correct Answer: Brother
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Hazrat Abu Hurayrah, has quoted Prophet Muhammadﷺ, as saying:

      "Do you know what backbiting is?" They said, "Allah and His Messenger know best." He then said, "It is to say something about your brother that he would dislike." Someone asked him, "But what if what I say is true?" Allah’s Messenger said, "If what you say about him is true, you are backbiting him, but if it is not true then you have slandered him."

      90

      In which Surah of the Holy Quran, the word "Khatimun Nabiyen" is mentioned?

      Correct Answer: Al Ahzab
      Show explanation
      Explanation



      Al-Ahzab is the 33rd chapter of the Quran with 73 verses.

      It is named after the confederates who fought the Muslims at the Battle of the Trench.

      It is also known as the Battle of the Parties or the Siege of Madina.

      91

      Hazrat Ayub (AS) was famous for his _____?

      Correct Answer: Patience
      Show explanation
      Explanation

       Ayub (Alayhi Salam) was the prophet of Allah during the era between Musa and Yaqub Prophet.

      The Prophet Ayub (peace be upon him) was one of the descendants of the Prophet Ibrahim (peace be upon him) 

      a nephew of Prophet Ya'qub (peace be upon him).

      Prophet Job had a lot of property in the land of Ash-Sham, money, livestock, and many children and servants.

      Hazrat Ayub A.S. – A prophet who was tested with Skin Disease for 18 Years ·

      The stories of Prophets of Islam Allah had sent down a total of 124,000 Prophets 

      Hazrat Loot (AS) was the materianl grandfather of Hazrat Ayub (AS)

      **
      ND13-1-2023


      92

      What is the title of Hazrat Ibrahim (A.S)?

      Correct Answer: Khalil Ullah
      Show explanation
      Explanation

       Khalilullah means friend of God

      Khalil Ullah is the title of Hazrat Ibrahim (A.S)

      Hazrat Ibrahim (A.S) was born Iraq

      Namrud ordered throwing Hazrat Ibrahim (A.S) into the fire

      Hazrat Ibrahim A.S remained on fire for 40 days

      Hazrat Ibrahim's (AH) name is mentioned in the Holy Quran 67 times.

      Abraham had two children,

      1.      Isaac

      2.      Ismael,


      Additional Information:


      Prophet Isa (Jesus) was known as 'Ruhullah' (i.e. the spirit of God) ,

      Prophet Musa (Moses) was known as 'Kalimullah' (i.e. one who speaks with God)


      AGM  5.2.23

      93

      Characteristics such as loyalty, honesty, respect, and sensitivity are examples of which of the following?

      Correct Answer: Values
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Characteristics like loyalty, honesty, respect, and sensitivity are examples of values.

      They are principles or standards of behavior that guide how individuals live and interact with others.

      94

      Being truthful is ______?

      Correct Answer: Virtuous
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Being truthful is considered virtuous.

      Because it reflects honesty and integrity, which are essential moral values.

      Truthfulness fosters trust and ethical behavior.

      95

      One of the Ethical standards is ____?

      Correct Answer: Being Truthful and Honest
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Being truthful and honest is a key ethical standard in any professional environment.

      It promotes trust and integrity.

      96

      Being empathetic to towards the problems of employees is _____?

      Correct Answer: Ethical
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Being empathetic towards the problems of employees is considered ethical.

      As it reflects understanding, care, and respect for others' well-being, which promotes a positive work environment.

      97

      Which of following the Quran considered as unpardonable sin?

      Correct Answer: Shirk
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      In Islam, shirk is the sin of idolatry or polytheism.

      Islam teaches that God does not share His divine attributes with any partner.

      98

      How many essential beliefs are there in Iman-e-Mafassal?

      Correct Answer: 7
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Seven essential beliefs are present in Iman-e-Mafassal

      1.  Faith in God.

      2.  Faith in holy angels.

      3.  Faith in all holy Books.

      4.  Faith in all holy Prophets.

      5.  Faith in the Day of Judgement.

      6.  Faith in everything good or bad is decided by God (Taqdir).

      7.  Faith in life after death..

      ND28-4-2023


      99

      Name the place where Muslims took refuge 7th year of Prophethood?

      Correct Answer: Shib-e-abi Talib
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      Shi'b Abi Talib is a valley between the Mount Abu Qubays and the Mount Khandama in Mecca.
      100

      Hazrat Hamza is _________ of Muhammad (PBUH)?

      Correct Answer: Uncle
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Hazrat Hamza Died: on 22 March 625; Mount Uhud, Medina, Arabia

      what is the relationship of Hazrat Hamza With Hazrat Muhammad (PBUH)?

      Answer: Uncle

      ***

      When did Hazrat Hamza embrace Islam?
      Fifth Nabvi
      ***
      Ameer Hamza (R.A) was martyred in __________ Ghazwa.
      Uhad
      ***

      Hamza ibn Abdul Muttalib Paternal Uncle of 

      Hazrat Muhammad.

      ***

      ND13-1-2023

      101

      Surah Al-Kahf means _____?

      Correct Answer: The Cave
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The word "kahf" means "the cave in the mountain"

      the sura is named "kahf" because it speaks about the story of the Companions of the Cave

      Qitmeer is the name of the dog in Surah Kahf.

      while

      The cave of Ashab e Kahaf is located in Jordan.

      Ashab e Kahf slept for 309 years

      The number of Ashab-e-Kahf was 7

      The story of Ashab al-Kahf is narrated in the eighteenth chapter of the Quran, called “al-Kahf”  In verses 9-26


      ****

      RY30012023

      ND5-2-2023ND10-3-2023

      SH26-8-2023

      102

      To whom Holy Prophet (PBUH) gave the title of Sayyidat Nisa al-Alamin?

      Correct Answer: Hazrat Fatima (R.A)
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Holy Prophet (PBUH) gave the title of "Sayyidat Nisa al-Alamin" (Leader of the Women of the Worlds) to Hazrat Fatima (R.A), his daughter.

      Hazrat Fatima (R.A) is considered a role model for Muslim women and is revered for her piety, wisdom, and courage.

      She was the wife of Hazrat Ali (R.A) and the mother of Hazrat Hasan (R.A) and Hazrat Hussain (R.A).

      103

      Which of the following is not one of the four Imam of the Sunni school of thought?

      Correct Answer: Imam Jaffer Sadiq (R.A)I
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The terminological meaning of 'Fiqh is "deep understanding"

      Fiqh refers to the body of Islamic law extracted from detailed Islamic sources 


      There are the following Five Schools of Islamic Thought.

      1. Hanafi
      2. Maliki
      3. Shafi΄i
      4. Hanbali

      ND19-3-2023

      104

      The Holy Prophet offered first Jumma prayer in ______?

      Correct Answer: 1 A.H
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Holy Prophet offered 1st Juma Prayer in 1st A.H

      Salat-e-Juma became Farz in which city  Madina
      105

      The short period between two Sajda’s is called _____?

      Correct Answer: Jalsa
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      A Rakat is one complete cycle of standing (Qa'adah), bending (Rukoo), prostration (Sajdah), and subsequent sitting (Jalsa).

      It also includes standing up again in the Salat.
      106

      How many companions (s) are there with the Holy Prophet (PBUH) during the first journey of Yathrib?

      Correct Answer: 1
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      There they stayed for three days and three nights.

      On September 24, 622 (1st Hijri)  Hazrat Abu Bakar (AS) accompanied the Prophet(SAW) in the migration

      Qaswa (camel) which the Holy Prophet (SAW) was riding in the migration of Madina

      Muhammad ﷺ and Hazrat Abu Bakr hid inside a cave on the Mount of Thawr.

      Surah Yaseen is recited by Hazrat Muhammad (PBUH) during migration

      ABOUT ABU BAKAR

      Abu Bakr Abdullah was a companion and, through his daughter Aisha.

      a father-in-law of the Islamic prophet Muhammad.

      first of the Rashidun Caliphs

      107

      Which city of Pakistan held the OIC Conference in 1997?

      Correct Answer: Islamabad
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      1st Extraordinary summit of OIC held in Islamabad between 23–24 March 1997    

      An Extraordinary OIC Summit was also held in Islamabad in 1997 on the occasion of Pakistan's Golden Jubilee.

      ***

      Pakistan hosted the Second OIC Summit Conference in February 1974 in Lahore.

      11th Session of The Islamic Summit Conference (Session of The Muslim Ummah in The 21St Century)

      Dakar, Senegal (13-14 March 2008)

      *******

      10th Session of The Islamic Summit Conference

      Putrajaya, Malaysia (16-17 October 2003)

      *******

      9th Session of The Islamic Summit Conference

      Doha, State of Qatar (12-13 November 2000)

      *******

      8th Session of The Islamic Summit Conference

      Tehran, Islamic Republic of Iran (9-11 December 1997)

      *******

      7th Session of The Islamic Summit Conference

      Casablanca, Kingdom of Morocco (13-15 December 1994)

      *******

      6th Session of The Islamic Summit Conference

      Dakar, Republic of Senegal (9 - 11 December 1991)

      *******

      5th Session of The Islamic Summit Conference

      Kuwait, State of Kuwait (26-29 January 1987)

      *******

      4th Session of The Islamic Summit Conference

      Casablanca, Kingdom of Morocco (16-19 January 1984)

      *******

      3rd Session of The Islamic Summit Conference

      Makkah Al-Mukarramah, Saudi Arabia (25-28 January 1981)

      *******

      2nd Session of The Islamic Summit Conference

      Lahore,  Islamic Republic of Pakistan (22 - 24 February 1974)

      *******

      1st Session of The Islamic Summit Conference

      Rabat, Kingdom of Morocco (September 1969)

      ******

      ND17-7-2023

           

      108

      Name the language in which the Holy Quran was translated for the first time in India?

      Correct Answer: Persian
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Quran was first translated into Persian in India

      It was part of the Arabic tafseer (exegesis) of the Quran written by Nizamuddin Nishpuri, an Iranian scholar.

      The Tafseer was named Tafseer-ul-Gharaaeb


      Additional information 

      The Holy Quran was first time translated into Urdu: Shah Abdul Qadir and Shah Rafiuddin (Mozah Al-Quran)

      The Holy Quran was first time translated into Punjabi: Tauqir Amaan Khan

      The Holy Quran was first time translated into Sindhi: Aakhund Azzizullah

      The Holy Quran first time translated into English: George Sale

      The Holy Quran was translated for the first time in World Persian

      109

      What is the First belief in Iman-e-Mufassal?

      Correct Answer: Tauheed
      Show explanation
      Explanation


      Muslims have seven beliefs:
      1 Belief in the Oneness of God.
      2 Belief in the Angels of God.
      3 Belief in the Revelations (Books) of God.
      4 Belief in the Prophets of God.
      5 Belief in the Day of Judgment.
      6 Belief in Premeasurement (Qadar).
      7 Belief in Resurrection after Death.
      110

      The first revelation of Surah Al-Qalam started with the word _____?

      Correct Answer: Nun
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The first revelation of Surah Al-Qalam (Chapter 68) started with the word "Nun".

      It is the 14th letter of the Arabic alphabet.

      The chapter begins with the verse: "Nun. By the pen and what they write." (Quran 68:1)

      111

      What are the second belief in Iman-e- Mufassal?

      Correct Answer: The angels of Allah
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Seven essential beliefs are there in Iman-e-Mafassal

      1.  Faith in God.

      2.  Faith in holy angels.

      3.  Faith in all holy Books.

      4.  Faith in all holy Prophets.

      5.  Faith in the Day of Judgement.

      6.  Faith in everything good or bad is decided by God (Taqdir).

      7.  Faith in life after death.

      112

      Who was the second Caliph?

      Correct Answer: Hazrat Umar(R.A.)
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      Who was the First Caliph?

      Hazrat Abu Bakar was the first caliph after Holy Prophet.

      Hazrat Abu Bakar remained Chalipha 8 June 632 – 23 August 634
      ***

      Who was the second Caliph?

      Hazrat Umar(R.A.)
      ***
      Who was the Third Caliph of Islam?

       Hazrat Usman(R.A.)
      ***


      Who was the fourth Caliph?

       Hazrat Ali (R.A.)

      Hazrat Ali(R.A.) Caliph period from 656 till 661 CE

      ***

      Which sahabi proposed the name of Hazrat Abu Bakr (R.A) for the first Caliphate of Islam?

      Hazrat Umar (R.A)

      ND10-3-2023








      113

      The poet of the Prophet (PBUH) was ______?

      Correct Answer: Hazrat Hassan (RA)
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The first poet of Islam was Hassan Bin Sabit.

      An Arabian poet and companion of the Prophet Muhammad SAW.

      He was renowned for his poetry defending and promoting Islam.

      He was Born in 563 AD.

      He was died in 674 AD, aged 110 or 111.

      114

      Belief in Tauheed is comprehensively described in _____?

      Correct Answer: Al Ikhlas
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Surah Al-Ikhlas (Chapter 112 of the Holy Quran) is a comprehensive description of the belief in Tauheed (the Oneness of God).

      It is a short chapter consisting of only 4 verses.

      But it provides a clear and concise explanation of the concept of Tauheed.

      It emphasizing the unity and uniqueness of Allah (God).


      Tawheed means the Oneness of Allah. No one likes worship without Allah.

      ***

      there are five pillars of Islam Tawheed is the first pillar 

      ***

      The Five Pillars of Islam

      The profession of Faith (shahada) Tawheed. The belief that "There is no god but God, and Muhammad is the Messenger of God" is central to Islam. 

      Prayer (salat). Muslims pray facing Mecca five times a day: at dawn, noon, mid-afternoon, sunset, and after dark

      Alms (zakat). 

      Fasting (sawm).

      Pilgrimage (hajj)

      115

      The language of the Holy Qur'an is _____?

      Correct Answer: Living
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The language of the Holy Qur'an is living.

      It is in Arabic, which remains a widely spoken and understood language.


      The Holy Quran was revealed to Hazrat Muhammad (PBUH).

      Iqra (meaning "Read") was the first word revealed in the Holy Quran.

      The first 5 verses of Surah Al-Alaq were revealed to the Holy Prophet (PBUH).

      Surah Al-Alaq contains the first revelation in the Holy Quran.

      Surah Al-Nasr was the last Surah revealed.

      The Quran was revealed orally by God through the archangel Gabriel (Jibril).

      The revelation of the Quran began in 610 CE when Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) was 40 years old.

      The Quran was revealed over 23 years, concluding in 632 CE, the year of the Prophet’s death.

      116

      The true purpose of Jehad is ______?

      Correct Answer: Glory of divine religion
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • The true purpose of Jihad is the glory of divine religion.
      • It involves striving in the way of Allah to promote justice, righteousness, and uphold the values of Islam.
      • To Strive Hard is the literal meaning of Jihad
      • ***
      • Jihad becomes first in.2AH
      • 41 times the word jihad appears in Quran
      • Jihad is an Arabic word derived from Jahada. 
      117

      Hazrat Jibrael used to come in the guise of ______?

      Correct Answer: Hazrat Dahya Kalbi (RA)
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Hazrat Jibrael used to come in the guise of Hazrat Dahya Kalbi.

      He was a companion of the Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) known for his handsome appearance, which Jibrael sometimes took when visiting the Prophet SAW.

      118

      Hazrat Amina mother of Muhammad (PBUH) was buried in _____?

      Correct Answer: Abwa
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Amina bint Wahb ibn Abd Manaf was the mother of the Islamic Prophet Muhammad SAW.

      She belonged to the Banu Zuhra tribe.


      119

      The grave of the Prophet was prepared by ?

      Correct Answer: Hazrat Abu Talha R.A
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The grave of the Holy Prophet Muhammad was prepared by a companion Hazrat Abu Talha RA.

      Prophet Hazrat Muhammad (PBUH) was buried in Madina (571 AD) 

      He Died  on June 8, 632 on Monday at the age of 63 in the city of Madina.

      120

      Who was the bestowed with both Prophethood and Kingship?

      Correct Answer: Hazrat Dawood (A.S)
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Hazrat Dawood (A.S) is a prophet and messenger of God (Allah) ,

      He was the second king of the United Kingdom of Israel and Judah, reigning c. 1010–970 BCE.


      Additional information

      Najibullah  is the title of Hazrat Dawood (AS) 

      The holy book Zaboor was revealed to Hazrat Dawood (AS)

      Hazrat Dawood (AS) is buried in Israel.

      Hazrat Dawood is called caliph of Allah.

      Hazrat Dawood (AS) is mentioned in the Holy Quran for 16 times. 

      121

      Which Manasiq of Hajj is Compulsory?

      Correct Answer: Waqf at Arafah (standing at Arafat)
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Pillar 3 – Waqfat Arafah

      The standing at 'Arafah (Wuquf Arafah) is a pillar of Hajj and is therefore compulsory.
      Additional information 

       Hajj means to intend.

      Hajj was made compulsory in 9 A.H.

      Hajj was ordained in Surah Bakr.

      There are 3 types of Hajj.

      The number of Jamarat is 3.

      The mosque located in Mina is Kheef.

      At Meekat, Hujjaj assumes the state of Ihram.

      Kalima Tauheed is recited during the Hajj.




      122

      What are the 4 Holy Books of Prophets?

      Correct Answer: see the short question and answer
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      There are four Holy Books

      1. The Torat  revealed to Musa
      2. The Zabur revealed to Dawud 
      3. The Injil (Gospel) revealed to Isa 
      4. The Quran was revealed to Muhammad.
      123

      Hazrat Muhammad ﷺ arranged education for whom due to his intellectual level?

      Correct Answer: Hazrat Hafsa (RA)
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Prophet Muhammad (ﷺ) recognized Hazrat Hafsa's (RA) intelligence and arranged for her education. 

      Hazrat Hafsah was the 4th wife of Hazrat Muhammad.

      At the time of the marriage, she was 22 years old. Muhammad was 56 years old.

      Hazrat Muhammad ﷺ married Hafsah in Shaaban 3AH 

      She was married to Khunais ibn Hudhaifah but became a widow 

      She was the daughter of Hazrat Umar (2nd Caliph)

      She was the First Woman “Hafiz e Quran”
      124

      Which Prophet proposed Azan for the first time?

      Correct Answer: see the answer in detial
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Hazrat 'Umar was the first person to suggest the calling of the Azan.

      125

      Which Islamic month has Shab-e-Mairaj?

      Correct Answer: Rajab
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Miraj event took place on the 27th of Rajab in the 10th (621 A.D) year of Prophethood.

      Prophet Muhammad (SAW) was staying at the house of Umm Hani on the night of Miraj.

      That night, Angel Jibreel came to the Prophet (SAW) and took him on the journey of Israa and Miraj.

      During Miraj, Hazrat Adam and Hazrat Muhammad (SAW) met at the first heaven.

      The event is mentioned in Surah Bani Israel.

      It is also known as 'Isra and Mi'raj' or 'Shab-e-Meraj'. 

      • Isra: Night journey from Makkah to Masjid Al-Aqsa.
      • Mi’raj: Journey from Masjid Al-Aqsa to the heavens.

      Gifts of the 27th of Rajab:

      • The five prescribed daily prayers.
      • The last part of the chapter of Al-Baqara (Amanar-Rasulu).
      126

      Who conquered the last fort of Khyber?

      Correct Answer: Hazrat Ali bin Abu Talib (A.S)
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Battle of Khyber fought against Jews of Khyber.

      Battle of Khyber began in Muharram on 7/628.

      Hazrat Ali killed Marhab while conquering the Qamus fort.

      127

      Which companion of the Holy Prophet (SAW) was called Al-Zulifqar?

      Correct Answer: Hazrat Ali (RA)
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Hazrat Ali (RA) was given the title "Al-Zulfiqar" by the Holy Prophet (SAW), which means "the possessor of the two swords".

      He was a cousin and son-in-law of the Prophet (SAW).

      He was known for his bravery and military prowess.

      The sword "Zulfiqar" was also given to him by the Prophet (SAW).

      He is considered a symbol of his valor and strength.

      128

      What was the name of the Abbasid Caliph who commissioned the translation of Greek philosophical works into Arabic?

      Correct Answer: Al-Mamun
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Al-Mamun (786-833 CE) was the seventh Abbasid Caliph, who ruled from 813 to 833 CE.

      He was a patron of learning and philosophy, and his court was renowned for its intellectual and cultural achievements.

      Al-Mamun commissioned the translation of many Greek philosophical works into Arabic, including the works of Aristotle, Plato, and other prominent philosophers.

      129

      According to Hanafi jurisprudence, what is the status of a marriage contract concluded without the presence of witnesses?

      Correct Answer: Invalid
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      In Hanafi jurisprudence, a marriage contract concluded without the presence of witnesses is considered valid.

      The Hanafi school of thought holds that the presence of witnesses is not a essential condition for the validity of a marriage contract.

      130

      Who among the following Islamic philosophers argued for the eternity of the universe?

      Correct Answer: Ibn Rushd
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Ibn Rushd (1126-1198 CE) argued for the eternity of the universe in his work "The Incoherence of the Incoherence".

      He believed that the universe has always existed and will always exist, with no beginning or end.

      This idea was controversial in his time, as it seemed to contradict the Islamic doctrine of creation.

      131

      Which Islamic theological school emphasized the role of reason in understanding divine commands?

      Correct Answer: Mutazilisim
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Mutazilisim is an Islamic theological school that emerged in the 8th century CE, emphasizing the role of reason in understanding divine commands.

      They believed that human reason could be used to interpret the Quran and the Hadith (the sayings and actions of the Prophet Muhammad), and that individuals had the freedom to choose between good and evil.

      132

      Who among the following Islamic philosophers developed the theory of "The Eternity of the Universe"?

      Correct Answer: Ibn Rushd
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Ibn Rushd (1126-1198 CE) developed the theory of "The Eternity of the Universe" in his work "The Incoherence of the Incoherence".

      He argued that the universe has always existed and will always exist, with no beginning or end.

      This idea was controversial in his time, as it seemed to contradict the Islamic doctrine of creation.

      133

      What is the Islamic concept of the "Intercession" on the Day of Judgement?

      Correct Answer: Al-Shafaah
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Al-Shafaah (الشفاعة) is the Islamic concept of "Intercession" on the Day of Judgment.

      It refers to the idea that certain individuals, such as prophets, saints, and martyrs, will intercede on behalf of sinners to Allah (God) to forgive their sins and grant them entry into Paradise.

      134

      Which of the following Islamic scholars is known for his work on the principles of Islamic governance?

      Correct Answer: Al-Mawardi
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Al-Mawardi (d. 1058 CE) was a renowned Islamic scholar and jurist.

      He wrote extensively on the principles of Islamic governance, politics, and public administration.

      His most famous work, "Al-Ahkam al-Sultaniyya" (The Ordinances of Government), is a seminal text on Islamic political theory and governance.

      135

      What is the term for the Islamic concept of the "Preservation of faith"?

      Correct Answer: Hifz Al-Iman
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Hifz Al-Iman (حفظ الإيمان) is the Islamic concept of "Preservation of Faith" or "Protection of Faith".

      It refers to the idea that Allah (God) protects and preserves the faith of a believer.

      It ensuring that they remain on the right path and do not stray from the teachings of Islam.

      136

      Who is the author of the influential Islamic theological text "Al-Iqtisad Fi Al-I'tiqad"?

      Correct Answer: Al-Ghazali
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Al-Ghazali is the author of "Al-Iqtisad Fi Al-I'tiqad".

      It is an influential text on Islamic theology that explores the principles of Islamic belief and rationality in theology.

      137

      Which of the following Sufi orders emphasizes the importance of spiritual love and intimacy with Allah?

      Correct Answer: Chishti
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Chishti Order is a Sufi order that emphasizes the importance of spiritual love and intimacy with Allah.

      The Chishti's believe that love is the most powerful force in the universe and that it is the key to unlocking spiritual growth and self-realization.

      They emphasize the development of a personal relationship with Allah, characterized by love, devotion, and intimacy.


      The Chishti's also emphasize the importance of:

      • Ishq (spiritual love)
      • Muhabba (divine love)
      • Uns (intimacy with Allah)
      • Fana (annihilation of the self)
      138

      What is the Islamic concept the "Signs of the end of time"?

      Correct Answer: Al-Isharat
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Al-Isharat, also known as "The Signs of the End of Time", refers to the Islamic concept of the indications or portents that signal the approaching end of the world and the Day of Judgment.

      These signs are believed to be foretold by the Prophet Muhammad (SAW) and are based on hadiths (his sayings and actions).


      The Signs of the End of Time include events such as:

      • The appearance of the Dajjal (Antichrist)
      • The descent of Jesus Christ (Isa)
      • The rise of the Mahdi (a righteous leader)
      • The emergence of the Ya'juj and Ma'juj (Gog and Magog)
      • The sinking of the earth in the East and West
      • The appearance of the smoke
      • The rising of the sun from the West
      139

      Who among the following Islamic scholars developed the theory of "The Four Sources of Shariah"?

      Correct Answer: Al-Shafi
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Al-Shafi'i (d. 820 CE) was a renowned Islamic scholar and jurist who developed the theory of "The Four Sources of Shariah" (الشرعية الأربعة):

      1. Quran (القرآن)
      2. Hadith (الحديث)
      3. Ijma (الإجماع) - Consensus of the Muslim community
      4. Qiyas (القياس) - Analogical reasoning
      140

      What is the term for the Islamic concept of the "Infallibility of Prophets"?

      Correct Answer: Ismah
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Ismah (عصمة) is the Islamic concept of the "Infallibility of Prophets".

      It refers to the belief that prophets are protected from committing sins and errors in their teachings and actions.

      This concept is based on the idea that prophets are chosen by Allah (God) to convey His message to humanity, and therefore, they must be trustworthy and reliable.

      141

      What is the Islamic concept of the "Night Journey"?

      Correct Answer: Al-Isra
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Al-Isra' wal-Mi'raj is also known as the Night Journey.

      It refers to the Prophet Muhammad's (SAW) journey from Mecca to Jerusalem and then to heaven, where he met with previous prophets and received revelations from Allah (God).


      The Night Journey is believed to have taken place in 621 CE, during the Prophet's lifetime.

      The terms Al-Isra' and Al-Mi'raj are often used together to describe the entire event, which consists of two parts:

      • Al-Isra' (الاسراء): The journey from Mecca to Jerusalem, where the Prophet prayed at the Temple Mount.
      • Al-Mi'raj (المعراج): The ascension to heaven, where the Prophet met with previous prophets and received revelations.
      142

      Which of the following Islamic scholars is known for his work on the principles of Islamic economics?

      Correct Answer: Ibn Khaldun
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Ibn Khaldun (d. 1406 CE) was a renowned Islamic scholar, historian, and economist.

      He is widely recognized as one of the founders of modern economics.

      His work, "The Muqaddimah" (The Prolegomena), is considered a seminal text in the field of Islamic economics.

      143

      What is the term for the Islamic concept of the "Rightly guided caliphs"?

      Correct Answer: Al-Khulafa Al-Rashidun
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Al-Khulafa Al-Rashidun (الخلفاء الراشدون) is the Islamic term for the "Rightly Guided Caliphs", who were the first four caliphs in Islamic history.

      They are considered to be the most righteous and virtuous leaders in Islamic history, and their reign is often referred to as the "Golden Age" of Islam.


      The four Rightly Guided Caliphs are:

      • Abu Bakr al-Siddiq (632-634 CE)
      • Umar ibn al-Khattab (634-644 CE)
      • Uthman ibn Affan (644-656 CE)
      • Ali ibn Abi Talib (656-661 CE)
      144

      Who is the author of the influence Islamic philosophical text "Al-Futuhat Al-Makkiyyah"?

      Correct Answer: Ibn Arabi
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Ibn Arabi (d. 1240 CE) was a renowned Islamic philosopher, mystic, and scholar who wrote the influential text "Al-Futuhat Al-Makkiyyah" (The Meccan Revelations).

      This work is considered one of the most important in Islamic philosophy and Sufism.

      "Al-Futuhat Al-Makkiyyah" is a comprehensive treatise on Islamic philosophy, theology, and spirituality, covering topics such as:

      • The nature of God and the universe
      • The role of the prophet and the imam
      • The path to spiritual realization and gnosis
      • The unity of existence (wahdat al-wujud)
      145

      Which of the following Sufi orders emphasizes the importance of spiritual retreat?

      Correct Answer: Naqshband
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Naqshband order is a Sufi order that emphasizes the importance of spiritual retreat (khalwa) as a means of spiritual growth and self-purification.

      Naqshbandis believe that spiritual retreat allows the individual to focus on their inner self, detach from worldly attachments, and cultivate a deeper connection with God.

      The Naqshband order was founded by Baha-ud-Din Naqshband Bukhari (d. 1389 CE) and is known for its emphasis on:

      • Spiritual retreat (khalwa)
      • Silent contemplation (muraqaba)
      • Devotional love (ishq)
      • Inner purification (tazkiya)
      146

      What is the Islamic concept of the "Seven Internal Organs" mentioned in the Quran?

      Correct Answer: Al-Nafs
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      In Islamic theology, Al-Nafs (النفس) refers to the concept of the "Seven Internal Organs" mentioned in the Quran.

      Al-Nafs is often translated as "the self" or "the soul".

      It is believed to comprise seven internal organs or faculties that govern human behavior and spiritual growth.


      The seven internal organs are:

      1. Al-Nafs al-Ammarah (the commanding self)
      2. Al-Nafs al-Lawwamah (the reproaching self)
      3. Al-Nafs al-Mulhimah (the inspired self)
      4. Al-Nafs al-Mutmainnah (the tranquil self)
      5. Al-Nafs al-Radiyah (the pleased self)
      6. Al-Nafs al-Mardiyyah (the pleasing self)
      7. Al-Nafs al-Safiyyah (the pure self)
      147

      Who among the following Islamic theologians developed the theory of Acquired Knowledge?

      Correct Answer: Al-Ghazali
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Al-Ghazali (d. 1111 CE) developed the theory of Acquired Knowledge (ilm al-muktasab), which posits that knowledge is acquired through reason, experience, and reflection, in addition to revelation and scripture.

      He emphasized the importance of intellectual inquiry and critical thinking in understanding the nature of reality and the divine.

      148

      What is the term for the Islamic concept of the "Preservation of the Quran"?

      Correct Answer: Hifz
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Hifz refers to the Islamic concept of the "Preservation of the Quran".

      In Islamic theology, Hifz means that the Quran has been perfectly preserved and protected from any corruption, alteration, or distortion since its revelation to the Prophet Muhammad (SAW).

      This concept is based on the belief that Allah (God) has taken it upon Himself to safeguard the Quran, ensuring its integrity and authenticity for all time.

      149

      Which of the following Islamic scholars is known for his work on the principles of Islamic jurisprudence?

      Correct Answer: Al-Shafi
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Al-Shafi'i (d. 820 CE) was a renowned Islamic scholar and jurist.

      He is known for his work on the principles of Islamic jurisprudence, particularly in his book "Al-Risala" (The Treatise).

      Al-Shafi'i's contributions to Islamic jurisprudence include:

      • Developing the concept of "usul al-fiqh" (principles of jurisprudence)
      • Establishing the importance of "ijma" (consensus) and "qiyas" (analogical reasoning) in legal decision-making
      • Clarifying the role of "hadith" (Prophetic traditions) in shaping Islamic law
      150

      What is the Islamic concept of the "Cycle of Prophecy"?

      Correct Answer: Nubuwaah
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Nubuwaah (Prophethood) refers to the Islamic concept of the "Cycle of Prophecy".

      It describes the process of divine revelation and guidance sent by Allah (God) to humanity through a series of prophets.

      This cycle begins with the first prophet, Adam, and continues through various prophets, including Abraham, Moses, Jesus, and culminates with the final prophet, Muhammad (SAW).

      151

      Who is the author of the influential Islamic theological text "Al-Muwafaqat"?

      Correct Answer: Al-Shatibi
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Al-Shatibi (d. 1388 CE) was a renowned Islamic scholar and theologian from Granada, Spain.

      He is best known for his influential work "Al-Muwafaqat fi Usul al-Sharia" (The Reconciliations in the Principles of Jurisprudence).

      It is a comprehensive treatise on Islamic theology, jurisprudence, and legal theory.

      152

      Which of the following Sufi orders emphasizes the importance of love and devotion to Allah?

      Correct Answer: Chishti
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Chishti Order is a Sufi order that emphasizes the importance of love and devotion to Allah.

      It was founded by Abu Ishaq Shami Chishti (d. 941 CE).

      He is known for its emphasis on spiritual purification, self-discipline, and love for the Divine.

      153

      What is the term for the Islamic concept of the "Collective Duty" to enforce moral and social justice?

      Correct Answer: Farz Kifayah
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Farz Kifayah is also known as Collective Duty.

      It refers to the Islamic concept where the community as a whole is responsible for ensuring that certain duties and obligations are fulfilled.

      This includes enforcing moral and social justice, promoting good, and preventing evil.

      If the community fails to fulfill these duties, individual members may be held accountable.

      154

      Which among the following Islamic philosophers is known for his theory of "Essential vs Accidental Existence"?

      Correct Answer: Ibn Sina
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Ibn Sina (980-1037 CE) was a renowned Islamic philosopher and scholar who developed the theory of "Essential vs Accidental Existence".

      This theory posits that existence can be categorized into two types:

      • Essential existence (wujud bi-dhatihi): This refers to the necessary existence of God, which is inherent and self-sustaining.
      • Accidental existence (wujud bi-ghayrihi): This refers to the contingent existence of created beings, which is dependent on God's will and sustenance.
      155

      According to Islamic jurisprudence, what is the primary source of Shariah Law?

      Correct Answer: Quran
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      In Islamic jurisprudence, the primary source of Shariah Law is the Quran.

      It is considered the word of God as revealed to the Prophet Muhammad (SAW).

      The Quran is the foundation of Islamic law.

      It is considered the ultimate authority in all matters of faith and practice.

      156

      Which of the following concepts is central to the Islamic doctrine of Tauheed?

      Correct Answer: Wahdat Al-Wujud
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The central concept of the Islamic doctrine of Tauheed (Tawhid) is the Oneness of Allah (Absolute Unity of God)





      Last updated by NA on 23-April-2026

      157

      What is the punishment for slander to a pious woman?

      Correct Answer: 80 Fogs
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      In Islamic law, the punishment for slander (known as "Qadhf" in Arabic) against a pious woman is 80 lashes.

      This is based on the Quranic verse:

      "And those who accuse chaste women and then do not bring four witnesses, flog them, (giving) eighty lashes, and do not admit any evidence from them ever." (Quran 24:4)

      158

      ______ got the fifth place in the history of the collectors of Hadith.

      Correct Answer: Tirmizi
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Imam Tirmizi (824-892 CE) was a renowned Islamic scholar and collector of hadith (Prophetic traditions).

      He compiled a hadith collection known as Jami' al-Tirmizi.

      It is considered one of the six most authentic hadith collections in Islam.

      Imam Tirmizi's work is ranked fifth in the history of hadith collection, after:

      1. Imam Bukhari (810-870 CE)
      2. Imam Muslim (817-875 CE)
      3. Imam Abu Dawud (817-889 CE)
      4. Imam Nasa'i (823-915 CE)

      So, Imam Tirmizi's collection is ranked fifth in the order of authenticity and preference.

      159

      Name the Ghazwa in which wine was prohibited?

      Correct Answer: Bani Nuzair
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Alcoholic drinks were banned in the fourth year of the Hijra (4 A.H), when Bani Nadeer Jews were driven into exile from their lands.

      160

      When Khalid bin Waleed was removed from the service in the reign of Hazrat Umar Farooq (رضہ)?

      Correct Answer: 17 A.H
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Khalid ibn al-Walid was an Arab Muslim commander in the service of the Islamic prophet Muhammad and the caliphs Abu Bakr and Umar .

      Born: 592 AD, Mecca, Saudi Arabia
      Died:       642 AD, Homs‎, Syria
      Buried:       642 AD, Khaled Ibn Walid Mosque, Homs‎, Syria
      161

      Who establishes Bait Ul Mal?

      Correct Answer: Hazrat Umar (RA)
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Bayt al-mal in Arabic term can be translated as “House of Money” or “House of Wealth.”

      The Bayt al-Mal was formally established during the second Caliph, Umar al-Khattab

      Hazrat Umar appointed Abdullah Bin Masud as custodian of Bait Ul Mal

      while 

      Pakistan Bait-ul-Mal (PBM), is an autonomous body set up through the 1991 Act.

      ND11-2-2023

      162

      Siege of Taif was laid in which of the following Hijri?

      Correct Answer: 8 A.H
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Siege of Taif was a military expedition led by the Prophet Muhammad (Peace Be Upon Him) in the year 8 A.H. (629-630 CE).

      The siege was an attempt to capture the city of Taif, which was a strategic location in Arabia, but it was unsuccessful.

      Despite the failure, the expedition marked an important turning point in the Prophet's military campaigns and the spread of Islam.

      163

      By whom were the Bani Israel prevented by Allah (SWT)?

      Correct Answer: Pharaoh
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      According to Islamic belief, Allah (SWT) rescued the Bani Israel from the tyranny of Pharaoh, who had enslaved them.

      Through miracles, including parting the Red Sea, Allah (SWT) allowed them to escape his oppression.

      164

      "Muhammad is the messenger of Allah" is stated in Surah _____?
