      Correct Answer: Surah Al-Fath
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • "Muhammad is the Messenger of Allah; and those with him are forceful against the disbelievers, merciful among themselves." - Surah Al-Fath, verses 29.
      • The name ‘Muhammad‘ is mentioned four times in the Holy Quran: Aal Imraan 3:144, Al-Ahzaab 33:40, Muhammad 47:2, and Al-Fatah 48:29.
        • The name ‘Ahmad‘ is mentioned once in Al-Saff 61:6.
        • Al-Fath, the 48th chapter of the Quran with 29 verses.
        • It was revealed in Madinah in the sixth year of Hijrah, on the occasion of the Treaty of Hudaybiyyah.
        165

        Holy Prophet used to go to a Mosque on every 17th Ramadan, state the name of Mosque?

        Correct Answer: Quba Mosque
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        The Holy Prophet (PBUH) used to visit Quba Mosque on the 17th of Ramadan.

        It holds significance as the first mosque built in Islam.

        It is located in Medina.

        166

        According to the teachings of Quran how many witnesses are required for the financial affairs of transaction?

        Correct Answer: Two Men
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        The Quran specifies that for financial transactions, "two men" should serve as witnesses. 

        Alternative option: If two men are not available, the requirement can be fulfilled by "one man and two women". 

        167

        Which term is used for the longest Ayah (Verse) of the Holy Quran?

        Correct Answer: Ayat E Mudayana
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Surah Al Baqrah is the longest Surah of Holy Quran.

        Ayat E Mudayana of Al-Baqara is the longest verse at the longest chapter in Quran.

        168

        How many clauses did the treaty of Hudaybiyyah contain?

        Correct Answer: 7
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Al-Hudaibiyah Treaty was scribed by Ali Al-Murtaza (R.A).

        The Treaty of Hudaybiyyah was an event that took place during the time of the Islamic prophet Muhammad.

        It was a pivotal treaty between Muhammad, representing the state of Medina, and the Qurayshi tribe of Mecca 

        Sulah e Hudaibiya is called Fatah Al-Mubeen in Surah Al-Fatah

        Sulah e Hudaibiya Was Signed In The Year 628/ 6H.

        Sule e Huabaibiya Was Signed For 10 Years.

        The Treaty of Hudaybiyyah took place in March 628 Dhu al-Qi’dah

        7 clauses did the treaty of Hudaybiyyah contain

        Hudabia is the name of the tree.

        Hazrat Muhammadﷺ, representing the state of Medina

        Suhail bin Amar signed the Treaty of Hudaibiya on behalf of the Quraish Mecca.

        ND-21-5-2023

        169

        During Ghazwa ____ to Tayammum was revealed?

        Correct Answer: Banu Mustaliq
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Tayammum is the Islamic act of dry Ritual purification using purified sand or dust

        The verse of Tayammum (dry ablution) was revealed during Battle of Banu Al-Mustaliq


        in Al-Ma'idah contains the order of wuzu, Ghusal, and Tayammum

        Tayammum is valid only for one obligatory Salah

        Tayammum is obligatory on 4 (AH)

        while 

        Wazu is obligatory on 5(AH)

        ND23-6-2023



        170

        When the Holy Quran First Translated into Latin?

        Correct Answer: 1143 AD
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        In 1143 Robert of Ketton produced the first Latin translation of the Qur'an.

        This translation, extant in 24 manuscripts

        while 

        The Holy Quran was first time translated into Urdu:

        Shah Abdul Qadir and Shah Rafiuddin (Mozah Al-Quran)

        The Holy Quran was first time translated into Punjabi:

        Tauqir Amaan Khan

        The Holy Quran was first time translated into Sindhi:

        Aakhund Azzizullah

        The Holy Quran first time translated into English:

        George Sale

        Holy Quran was translated for the first time in India

        Sindhi

        Holy Quran was translated for the first time in World

        Persian

        ND23-6-2023



        171

        Najashi became Muslim at the hand of ______?

        Correct Answer: Hazrat Jafar
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Najashi is also known as Ashama ibn Abjar.

        He was the King of Abyssinia (modern-day Ethiopia) who became a Muslim at the hands of Hazrat Jafar ibn Abi Talib, a cousin of the Prophet Muhammad (SAW).

        Hazrat Jafar ibn Abi Talib recited the Quran to Najashi, who was impressed by its beauty and truth, and subsequently accepted Islam.

        172

        When Hazrat Umar conquered Bait Ul Maqdas in ____ A.H.

        Correct Answer: 16 A.H
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Umar ibn Abd al-Aziz, commonly known as Umar II, was the eighth Umayyad caliph

        Umar ibn Abd al-Aziz called the first Mujaddid

        and sixth righteous caliph of Islam after Hasan Ibn Ali

        Umar ibn Abd al-Aziz was Born 2 November 682 in Medina, Umayyad Caliphate

        Died 5 February 720 (aged 37)

        Hazrat Umar conquered Bait Ul Maqdas in 16 A.H.

        ND18-5-2023
        173

        Who was the pupil of Hazrat Abu Hurairah (RA)?

        Correct Answer: Hazrat Hmmam ibn Munabih
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Hazrat Abu Hurairah (RA) was a companion of the Prophet Muhammad (SAW) and a renowned scholar.

        Among his pupils was Hazrat Hammam ibn Munabbih, who was a well-known scholar and narrator of hadith (Prophetic traditions).

        174

        How many Salats were made obligatory during the Mairaj?

        Correct Answer: 5
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Five prayers were declared compulsory in the 10th Nabvi

        Namaz commanded in Quran 700 times.

        Salat was made compulsory at the event of 27 Rajab ( Miraj )

        ****

        Salat Juma became Farz in Madina.

        Hajj was made compulsory in the 09th Hijri.

        175

        What was the relationship between Imam Bukhari and Imam Hanbal?

        Correct Answer: Teacher - Student
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Imam Bukhari (810-870 CE) was a renowned Islamic scholar and compiler of the hadith collection "Sahih al-Bukhari".

        Imam Ahmad ibn Hanbal (780-855 CE) was a prominent Islamic scholar and founder of the Hanbali school of thought.

        Imam Bukhari was a student of Imam Ahmad ibn Hanbal.

        He attended Imam Hanbal's lectures and learned from him.

        Imam Bukhari is said to have greatly respected Imam Hanbal and considered him as one of his teachers.

        176

        حضرت عیسیٰ علیہ السلام کا معجزہ کیا تھا؟

        Correct Answer: ان سب میں سے سب
        Show explanation
        Explanation

         حضرت عیسیٰ علیہ السلام کے معجزات میں نابینا کو بینا کرنا، کوڑھ میں مبتلا لوگوں کو ٹھیک کرنا اور مردوں کو زندہ کرنا شامل ہیں۔


        177

        ام القریٰ کس شہر کو کہا جاتا ہے؟

        Correct Answer: مکہ مکرمہ
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        مکہ مکرمہ کو "ام القریٰ" کہا جاتا ہے کیونکہ یہ دنیا کے سب سے اہم اور مقدس شہروں میں سے ایک ہے اور اسلامی تاریخ کا اہم مرکز ہے۔


        178

        حضرت موسیٰ علیہ السلام نے کس امت میں ہونے کی خواہش ظاہر کی؟

        Correct Answer: حضرت محمد صلی اللہ علیہ وسلم امت
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        حضرت موسیٰ علیہ السلام نے حضرت محمد صلی اللہ علیہ وسلم کی امت میں ہونے کی خواہش ظاہر کی کیونکہ اس امت کو فضیلت دی گئی۔

        179

        Hajr-e-Aswad is installed on the wall of Kaaba. What is the meaning of Aswad?

        Correct Answer: Black
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Aswad means کالا یا سیاہ رنگ کا 

        Hajr-e-Aswad is a rock set into the eastern corner of the Kaaba, the ancient building in the center of the Grand Mosque in Makkah.

        180

        Which battle led to the birth of the Khawarij?

        Correct Answer: Battle of Siffin
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        The Khawarij sect emerged as a result of the Battle of Siffin (657 CE).

        It was fought between the forces of Caliph Hazrat Ali and those of Muawiyah ibn Abu Sufyan. 

        The battle ended in a stalemate, and the arbitration process that followed led to a group of rebels, who would later become known as the Khawarij, to break away from Hazrat Ali's camp.

        181

        What is Jobbery?

        Correct Answer: Conduct of public business for private gain
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Jobbery is a noun that refers to the act of using one's public office or position for personal gain or benefit, often through corrupt or unethical means.

        This can include:

        • Using one's position to secure contracts or business deals for personal benefit
        • Accepting bribes or kickbacks
        • Misusing public funds or resources for personal gain
        • Engaging in nepotism or favoritism
        182

        The great book "Al-Itqan fi Uloom al-Quran" was composed by whom?

        Correct Answer: Jalal al-Din Suyati
        Show explanation
        Explanation
        • Al-Itqan fi Uloom al-Quran was composed by Jalal uddin Suyati.
        • He was a scholar and writer.
        • In this book, he explained the revelation time of Surahs and Ayats of the Holy Quran.
        183

        Which Prophet of Allah had minimum age?

        Correct Answer: Hazrat Easa (AS)
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Hazrat Easa (AS) was around 33 years old.

        Hazrat Ibrahim (AS) was around 195 years old.

        Hazrat Muhammad (SAW) was around 63 years old.

        Hazrat Musa (AS) was around 125 years old.

        Hazrat Adam (AS) was around 1000 years old.

        184

        Which Muslim Caliph had all his four sons caliph too?

        Correct Answer: Abdul Malik bin Marwan
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Abdul Malik bin Marwan (5th Umayyad Caliph)

        Had 4 sons who became Caliphs:

        1. Al-Walid I
        2. Sulayman
        3. Yazid II
        4. Hisham
        185

        Among the following Prophets, whose nation was awarded with the Mann-o-Salwa?

        Correct Answer: Hazrat Musa (AS)
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Mann-o-Salwa is a type of food that was gifted to Bani Israel (people of Prophet Musa A.S).

        There were two foods:

        • One was “Mann” and the other was “Salwa”. 
        • Mann was a kind of sweet
        • Salwa was a kind of Bird
        186

        Name the first foster mother of the Prophet Muhammad (PBUH)?

        Correct Answer: Hazrat Sobia (RA)
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        The first foster mother of Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) was Hazrat Sobia (RA)

        Hazrat Sobia (RA) was a slave woman of Abu Lahab.

        She nursed the Prophet (PBUH) after his birth before he was later cared for by Hazrat Haleema (RA).

        187

        The Holy Quran contains the Makki and Madni Surah’s. The Madni surah’s have a general emphasis on _____?

        Correct Answer: Social & Religious laws & values
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        The Madni Surahs, revealed in Madina, focus on:

        • The social and religious laws
        • Values for the Muslim community
        • Including rules for personal and collective behavior, worship, and relationships.


        Makki Surahs, revealed in Mecca, focus on:

        • The Oneness of God (Tawheed)
        • The Hereafter (Akhirah)
        • The Prophethood of Muhammad (PBUH)
        • The struggle against polytheism and disbelief
        • The basic principles of faith and morality
        188

        How many times the mother of Hazrat Ismail (AS) ran from Hill of Safa and Marwa in search of Water?

        Correct Answer: 7
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Hazrat Hajra, the mother of Hazrat Ismail (AS), ran between the hills of Safa and Marwa seven times in search of water.

        This act is commemorated during the Hajj pilgrimage, specifically in the ritual of Sa'i.

        The running b/w Safa and Marwa seven times is called Sayee.

        189

        خطیب الانبیاء کس کا لقب ہے؟

        Correct Answer: see the answer
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        خطیب الانبیاء کا لقب حضرت شعیب علیہ السلام کو دیا گیا ہے۔

        • حضرت شعیب علیہ السلام: آپ بنی مدین کی طرف بھیجے گئے نبی تھے۔
        • پیغام: آپ نے اپنی قوم کو اللہ کی عبادت کرنے، بے انصافی اور دھوکہ دہی سے بچنے کی تلقین کی۔
        • خطابت کی خصوصیت: آپ کی خطابت کی صلاحیت اور قوت بیان کی وجہ سے آپ کو "خطیب الانبیاء" کا لقب دیا گیا۔
        • قرآن میں ذکر: آپ کا ذکر مختلف آیات میں کیا گیا ہے، جہاں آپ کے پیغام اور قوم کی طرف آپ کی جدوجہد کو بیان کیا گیا ہے
        190

        Name the foster mother of Holy Prophet

        Correct Answer: see the answer
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Hazrat Halimah and her husband were from the tribe of Sa'd.

        3 foster mothers of the Holy Prophet

        1.        Hazrat Halema Saadia (RA)    
        2.      Hazrat Khola  (RA)
        3.        Hazrat Suwaiba (RA)
        191

        What are the 4 holy book of Prophet?

        Correct Answer: see the answer
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        There are four Holy Books

        1. The Torat  revealed to Musa
        2. The Zabur revealed to Dawud 
        3. The Injil (Gospel) revealed to Isa 
        4. The Quran was revealed to Muhammad.
        192

        To whom did The Holy Prophet(SWA) go immediately after the revelation of the first Wahi?

        Correct Answer: Hazrat Khadija (RA)
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        پہلی وحی کے وقت رسول اللہ صلی اللہ علیہ وسلم کی عمر کتنی تھی؟

        40 years 6 months

        When the first wahi was revealed to the Holy Prophet ﷺ?

        610AD

        ****


        What was the age of Rasool Ullah (PBUH) at the time of the first Wahi?

        40 years 6 months

        ********



        How many verses (Ayats) were in the first Wahi?

        5

        ********


        Name the first prescriber of wahi in Makkah.

        Sharjeel-ibn-Hasana Kandi

        ***

        Who was the first writer of “Wahi” in Quraish?
        Hazrat Zaid bin Sabit (RA)

        ********

        The first Wahi was descended on Cave Hira.

        پہلی وحی غارحرا میں نازل ہوئی تھی۔

        ****

        ND10-1-2023

        ***

        ND28-1-2023


        193

        Which surah is called “Babul Quran”?

        Correct Answer: Surah Fatiha
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Surah Fatiha (Chapter 1 of the Quran) is often referred to as "Babul Quran," meaning "The Gate of the Quran" or "The Opening of the Quran."

        It is the opening chapter.

        It is recited in every unit of prayer (rak'ah) in Muslim worship.


        Surah Fatiha is also known by several other names, including:

        Umm al-Kitab (Mother of the Book)

        Umm al-Quran (Mother of the Quran)

        Al-Sab'a al-Mathani (The Seven Oft-Repeated Verses)

        Al-Hamd (The Praise)

        As-Salah (The Prayer)

        Al-Fatiha al-Khabira (The Great Opening)

        Al-Fatiha al-'Udhma (The Mighty Opening)

        194

        Mujadid Alif Sani belong to _____?

        Correct Answer: Naqshbandī order
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Mujadid Alif Sani is the title of Sheikh Ahmad Sirhindi, an Indian Islamic scholar, Hanafi jurist, and member of the Naqshbandī Sufi order.

        He is known for countering unorthodox Sufism and mystic beliefs, with notable works including Isbat-ul-Nabat and Risal-i-Nabuwat.

        195

        Who was the first Muslim Nobel Prize laureate?

        Correct Answer: Anwar Sadaat
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        The Norwegian Nobel Committee awarded the Peace Prize in 1978 to Anwar al-Sadat

        The Nobel Peace Prize 1978 for their contribution to the two frame agreements on peace in the Middle East, and on peace between Egypt and Israel

        Muhammad Anwar el-Sadat was an Egyptian politician and military officer who served as the third president of Egypt, from 15 October 1970 until 6 October 1981

        Born: 25 December 1918, Mit Abu al-Kawm, Egypt

        Died: 6 October 1981, Cairo, Egypt

        NA21-8-2023

        196

        Order is the law of civilization as chaos is the law of the ______. The most appropriate word to be filled in is:

        Correct Answer: Wilderness
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Order is the law of civilization as chaos is the law of the Wilderness.


        This pairing contrasts the structured nature of civilization with the untamed aspects of the wilderness.

        197

        "Seek knowledge even if you have to go to ____."

        Correct Answer: China
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        The phrase "Seek knowledge even if you have to go to China" is a famous saying attributed to the Prophet Muhammad (SAW). 

        This saying emphasizes the importance of seeking knowledge and education, even if it requires traveling to distant lands.

        In this case, China was considered a far-off land, symbolizing the idea that one should be willing to go to great lengths to acquire knowledge.

        198

        Which Surah was revealed when the Prophet (PBUH) was taunted for having no son?

        Correct Answer: Surah Kausar
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Surah Kausar was revealed when the Prophet (PBUH) was taunted for not having a son.

        It is a message of comfort, promising abundant blessings and countering the taunts of his enemies.

        199

        What happened on 27 Rajab? MARKS 2

        Correct Answer: see the answer in detail
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        On 27th Rajab, the Islamic tradition commemorates Isra and Mi'raj, also known as the Night Journey and Ascension of the Prophet Muhammad (PBUH).


        200

        صورکون پھونکیں گے

        Correct Answer: حضرت اسرافیل علیہ السلام
        Show explanation
        Explanation

         حضرت اسرافیل علیہ السلام قیامت کے دن اللہ تعالیٰ کے حکم سے صور میں پھونک ماریں گے،

        جس کے نتیجے میں سب جاندار ختم ہو جائیں گے،

        اور پھر دوسری مرتبہ صور پھونکنے پر تمام مردے دوبارہ زندہ ہوں گے

        201

        Who is Abdullah ibne saba?

        Correct Answer: A Jewish convert accused of spreading fitna (discord) in early Islam
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Abd Allah ibn Saba was a Yemenite Jew from the Arab Himyar tribe who converted to Islam during Uthman's reign


        عبداللہ ابن سبا — ایک یہودی تھا جو بظاہر مسلمان ہوا اور ابتدائی اسلامی تاریخ میں فتنہ اور اختلاف پھیلانے کے حوالے سے مشہور ہے۔ بعض روایات کے مطابق اس نے حضرت علیؓ کی الوہیت کا عقیدہ بھی پھیلایا۔



        202

        _____ is called Sayeed-ul-Ayam?

        Correct Answer: Friday
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Friday is considered a sacred day in Islam.

        It is often referred to as "Sayeed-ul-Ayam".

        It means "the master/king of days".

        This is because Friday is considered a day of gathering, worship, and spiritual renewal for Muslims.

        It is marked by the congregational prayer (Jumu'ah) in mosques.
        203

        Al-Azhar Mosque was completed in which year?

        Correct Answer: 972 AD
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Al-Azhar Mosque is located in Cairo, Egypt.

        It was completed in 972 AD during the Fatimid dynasty.

        It is one of the oldest and most important mosques in the Islamic world.

        It is considered a center of Islamic learning and culture.

        204

        The portion of the wall of Kaba which is between its door and Hajr-e-Aswad is called ____?

        Correct Answer: Al-Multazim
        Show explanation
        Explanation


        Multazam is basically an area which is bordered by Hajr-e-Aswad on one side and the door of Kaaba on the other.

        Multazim is a part of Holy Kaaba
        205

        Which tribe was from the generation of Hazrat Ismaeel (A.S.)?

        Correct Answer: Banu Quraysh
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        The Banu Quraysh tribe, which is the tribe of the Prophet Muhammad (PBUH), is indeed descended from Hazrat Ismaeel (A.S.).

        • Hazrat Ismail (AS) is called 'Zabeeh Ullah' (Zabiullah).
          • Hazrat Ismail (A.S) was Son of Hazrat Ibrahim (A.S) & Hazrat Hajra (A.S).
          • Prophet Ismail (AS) and Prophet Ishaq (AS) are Brothers.


          206

          The father's name of Hazrat Yaqoob (A.S.)?

          Correct Answer: Hazrat Ishaq (A.S.)
          Show explanation
          Explanation
          • Yaqub was also known as Israel.
          • He is the son of Ishaq (father) and Rafqa (mother)‎
          • He was the grandson of Hazrat Ibrahim A.S.
          • Hazrat Ishaq was the younger brother of Hazrat Ismail A.S
          • He has 12 Children including Yusuf and Binyamin
          • His name mentioned 16 times in the Quran
          • He was born in Palestine,
          • He met his son Hazrat Yousaf (AS) after a period of 40 years
            • The Holy Qur'an has mentioned his story in a beautiful chapter entitled "Surah Yusuf".
          207

          How many Ansaar from Madina embraced Islam in 11th Nabvi year?

          Correct Answer: Six
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Six Ansaar from Madina embraced Islam in the 11th year of Nabvi during the Prophet Muhammad's (PBUH) time.

          They were among the first people from Madina to accept Islam and helped pave the way for the spread of Islam in the city.

          Hazrat Suwaid bin Samit R.A was the first person from Madina to accept Islam.

          208

          While reciting Kalima Shahadat, a Muslim takes oath that _____?

          Correct Answer: There is no god but Allah, and Muhammad (PBUH) is His Messenger
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Reciting the Kalima Shahadat, also known as the Shahada, a Muslim declares:

          "La ilaha illallah, Muhammadur rasulullah"

          Which translates to:

          "There is no god but Allah, and Muhammad (PBUH) is His Messenger"

          This declaration affirms the fundamental belief in Islam, affirming the oneness of Allah (Tawhid) and accepting Muhammad (PBUH) as the final Prophet and messenger of Allah.

          209

          Mention the category in which human beings will be divided on the Day of Judgement?

          Correct Answer: All of these
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          On the Day of Judgment, humanity will be divided into three main categories:

          1. The Believers (Al-Mu'minun): Those who believed in Allah and lived righteously.
          2. The Disbelievers (Al-Kafirun): Those who rejected Allah and His teachings.
          3. The Hypocrites (Al-Munafiqun): Those who pretended to be Muslims but lacked true faith.

          Each group's fate will be determined, with the Believers entering Paradise, the Disbelievers in Hell, and the Hypocrites facing severe punishment.

          210

          Name the person who shall step into Jannat first of all?

          Correct Answer: Holy Prophet Muhammad (PBUH)
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          According to Islamic belief, the Holy Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) will be the first person to enter Jannat (Paradise) on the Day of Judgment.

          This is based on various hadiths (sayings) of the Prophet Muhammad (PBUH), which state that he will be the first to enter Paradise.

          This followed by his companions and other righteous believers.

          211

          At what occasion all the six kalimahs are recited by the Muslims?

          Correct Answer: Nikah
          Show explanation
          Explanation
          • The Six Kalimas are broadly speaking taken from hadiths, now not from the Qur'an.
          • The Six Kalimahs (from Arabic كلمة‎ kalimah "word") in Islam in South Asia are six significant elements of 1's non secular perception, typically taken from hadiths (in some traditions, six phrases, then called the six kalimas).
          • six kalimas are recited in Nikah
          212

          Which Prophet's nation received the azab (punishment) of mosquitoes?

          Correct Answer: Hazrat Ibrahim (AS)
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Azab of Mosquitoes was sent to the nation of Hazrat Ibrahim (AS).

          It was directed specifically at the arrogant king Namrood (Nimrod) and his blasphemous army.

          213

          Where is Shaikh Abdul Qadir Jilani buried?

          Correct Answer: Baghdad
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Shaikh Abdul Qadir Jilani known as Muḥyī l-Dīn Abū Muḥammad
          Shaikh Abdul Qadir Jilani Born: March 23, 1078, Gilan Province, Iran
          Shaikh Abdul Qadir Jilani Died:           February 1166, Baghdad, Iraq
          Shaikh Abdul Qadir Jilani Place of burial:           The Holy Shrine Of Shekh Abdul Qadir Gilani, Baghdad, Iraq.
          214

          "And you see that people are entering Allah's religion in crowds" the following verse in the part of which surah?

          Correct Answer: Al-Nasr
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          The verse "And you see that people are entering Allah's religion in crowds" is from Surah Al-Nasr (Chapter 110, Verse 2).

          This surah is the 110th chapter of the Quran and has only 3 verses.

          It is a short surah that discusses the victory of Islam and the large numbers of people embracing the faith.

          Surah Al-Nasr is also known as the "Surah of Victory" or "Surah of Help".

          It is often recited as a du'a (supplication) for guidance and support.

          215

          During drought, which Namaz is performed?

          Correct Answer: Salaat al Istasqa
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Salat ul istasqa is a prayer to ask God for rain. 

          It consists of two Rakat.

          Salatul-Istisqaa is no Adhan or Iqamah – only recitation of the Quran in both rakats out loud. 

          Al-Istisqa'means seek water (rain) from Alla during times of low rainfall.

          *****

          while

          Namaz-e-Khasoof (نماز خسوف) is offered at the time of Lunar eclipse

           Namaz-e-Kasoof (نماز کسوف) is offered at the time of Solar eclipse


          ***

          ND27-12-2022

          ND03-12-2022


          ND22-6-2023

          216

          In which parah do we find Surah Al-Noor?

          Correct Answer: 18
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Surah Al-Noor is found in the 18th Parah (Juz) of the Quran.

          It has 64 verses.

          It is divided into 9 Rukus.

          It focusing on social laws and morality.

          217

          انتہا پسندی کے خاتمے کے لیے تعلیم کی کیا اہمیت ہے؟

          Correct Answer: The Guide
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          سوال 1: انتہا پسندی کے خاتمے کے لیے تعلیم کی کیا اہمیت ہے؟

          تعلیم انتہا پسندی کے خاتمے میں کلیدی کردار ادا کرتی ہے۔ جب لوگوں کو معیاری اور بامعنی تعلیم ملتی ہے، تو وہ فرقہ واریت، شدت پسندی، اور منفی نظریات سے آگاہ ہوتے ہیں۔ تعلیم فرد کو تنقیدی سوچ کی صلاحیت فراہم کرتی ہے، جو انتہا پسندانہ نظریات کی مخالفت میں مددگار ثابت ہوتی ہے۔ تعلیم سے عوام میں تنگ نظری کی بجائے تنوع کی قبولیت کی طرف رجحان بڑھتا ہے اور سماجی ہم آہنگی میں اضافہ ہوتا ہے۔

          218

          What is the meaning of Al Hadi mentioned in the Holy Quran?

          Correct Answer: The Guide
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          In the Holy Quran, "Al-Hadi" means "The Guide or The Way."

          It refers to Allah as the one who guides humanity to the right path and truth.

          219

          Who was the leader/spokesman of second migration to Habsha during Prophet's (SAW) stay in Makkah?

          Correct Answer: Jaafer ibn Abi Talib
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Jaafer ibn Abi Talib led the second migration to Habsha (Abyssinia).

          He acted as the spokesman when the Muslim refugees were questioned by the king, Najashi.


          In Surah, Anfal mentions the migration of the Holy Prophet

          The first migration to Habshah (Abyssinia) took place in 613 A.D

          The relatives of Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) in the first migration to Abyssinia were Usman Ghani (R.A) and Ruqaiyah (R.A)

          There were 12 men and 4 women in the first migration to Abyssinia

          *************

          The second migration to Habshah took place in 616 A.D

          The group of emigrants' second  migrations time comprised 83 men and 18 to 19 women


          ND5-2-2023

          220

          From which matter, Allah created the first human being?

          Correct Answer: Clay
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Allah created the first human being, Adam, from clay.

          In Surah Al-Hijr (15:26), it says: "And We did certainly create man out of clay from an altered black mud."

          221

          How many Tashdeed (تشديد) symbols are there in the Holy Quran?

          Correct Answer: 1274
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          : قرآن مجید میں تقریبا 53223 زبر

          ،تقریبا 39582 زیر

          ، تقریبا 1771 مد،

          تقریبا 8804 پیش،

          تقریبا 1274 تشدید،

          تقریبا 105684 نقاط

          تقریبا 86430 کلمات استعمال


          222

          Sir Creek is located between ______?

          Correct Answer: Pakistan & India
          Show explanation
          Explanation
          • Sir Creek is located between Pakistan & India
          • ***
          • Sir Creek is located along the southeastern border of the Sindh province of Pakistan and the western border of the Gujarat province in India.
          • This area is called the Greater Rann of Kutch, a marshland situated along the Arabian Sea.
          • ***
          • Sir Creek is important to Pakistan because: It is environmentally important to prevent coastal wetlands from degradation
          • About 90 km2 of the lake comes under Pakistan, whilst the majority of the lake i.e. 210 km2, lies within India.
          223

          The word 'Wahi' is derived from the word 'Waha' which means?

          Correct Answer: To inform secretly
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          The word "Wahi" is derived from the Arabic word "Waha".

          It means to convey a message secretly or to give an inspiration quietly and subtly.

          The literal meaning of "Wahi" is:  Allah's Message

          2 kinds of Wahi are there

          1 Wahee-e-Matlu:

          It means the revelation which God has transmitted in His own words and speech

          2 Wahee-e-Ghair Matlu:

          It is the revelation that Allah projected in the heart of the Holy Prophet.

          224

          After migrating to Yasrab (Madina) the Holy Prophet first stayed at the house of which sahabi?

          Correct Answer: Ayub Ansari
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          After migrating to Madina, the Holy Prophet (SAW) first stayed at the house of Hazrat Ayub Ansari (R.A).

          Abu Ayyub al-Ansari أبو أيوب الأنصاري

          The patronym Abu Ayyub, means father (abu) of Ayyub.

          Abu Ayyub died of illness during the First Arab Siege of Constantinople.

          Born Yathrib, Hejaz, Arabia

          Died c. 674 Constantinople, Byzantine Empire

          Relations Banu Khazraj (Banu Najjar

          225

          The action or saying in the front of Holy Prophet Mohammad (SAW) on which Holy Prophet Mohammad (SAW) kept silence is called?

          Correct Answer: Hadith Taqreeri
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Taqreer refers to the Holy Prophet's (SAW) silent approval or consent to a statement or action.

          Hadith is the Saying (قول), Action (عمل), and Taqrer (تقرير)  of Prophet Mohammad (SAW).

          There are three types of Ahaddis:

          1. Saying
          2. Action
          3. Taqreer
          226

          Which child of Hazrat Muhammad (S.A.W) passed away first?

          Correct Answer: Hazrat Qasim
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Hazrat Qasim was the first (elder) son of Hazrat Muhammad ﷺ and Hazrat Khadijah

          He died at the age of only 3 and was buried in Jannat al Mualla in Makkah.


          Additional Information:

          Prophet Muhammad ﷺ had three sons and four daughters.

          1. Hazrat Qasim 
          2. Hazrat Zainab (RA)
          3. Hazrat Ruqayyah (RA) 
          4. Hazrat Umm Kulthum (RA) 
          5. Hazrat Fatima (RA) 
          6. Hazrat Abdullah
          7. Hazrat Ibrahim 

          All of his children were born to Hazrat Khadijah (RA) except one son Ibrahim, who was born to Hazrat Maria al-Qibtiyah (RA).
          227

          The real name of Umm-ul-Momineen “Bibi Umm-e-Habiba” is _____?

          Correct Answer: Ramla
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Umm-ul-Momineen Bibi Umm-e-Habiba

          Wife of

          The Holy Prophet (PBUH)

          Real Name

          Ramla

          Daughter of

          Abu Sufyan

          Umm-ul-Momineen

          Mother of believers

          228

          Name of first biggest lair who announced the false Prophet-hood?

          Correct Answer: Muslima kazab
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Muslima  kazab      مسلمہ کذاب

          He claim false Prophet-hood in the life of Muhammad 

          229

          After the Battle of Yarmouk, which was won by the Muslims Khalid bin Waleed was removed by the Caliph Umar (RA) from his supreme command. The new Supreme Commander was:

          Correct Answer: Abu Ubaydah (RA)
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Abu Ubaidah ibn al-Jarrah, fully Abū 'Ubaydah 'Āmir ibn 'Abdillāh ibn al-Jarāḥ was one of Companions of the Islamic prophet Muhammad.
          230

          When Hazrat Umar (R.A) assumed the caliphate?

          Correct Answer: 13 A.H
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Hazrat Umar (R.A) was the second caliph of Islam from 13 A.H to 23 AH (around 10 years)

          231

          Ethics is about ______?

          Correct Answer: Concepts of Right and Wrong
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Ethics is the branch of philosophy that deals with principles of morality.

          It helping individuals distinguish between right and wrong actions.

          232

          What is the meaning of Surah Nahl?

          Correct Answer: Honey bee
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Surah An Nahl is the 16th chapter of the Holy Quran which means شہد کی مکھی

          233

          First Mujadid was Hazrat Umar bin Abdul Aziz. Who was the second one?

          Correct Answer: Ahmad Sirhindi
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Aḥmad al-Fauqi al-Sirhindi is second is a second Mujahid

          He is also known as Imam Rabbani

          He was born on 26 June 1564 

          died on 10 Dec 1624.

          He is also known as Mujadad Alf-Sani.


          Additional information

          Umar ibn Abd al-Aziz, commonly known as Umar II

          He was the eighth Umayyad caliph

          He called the first Mujaddid

          He was born 2 November 682 in Medina

          Died on 5 February 720 

          Hazrat Umar conquered Bait Ul Maqdas in 16 A.H.


          ND18-5-2023



          234

          Tarikh-ul-rusul wal-al-Muluk is written by _____?

          Correct Answer: At'tibri
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          At'tibri is the author of "Tarikh-ul-Rusul wal-Al-Muluk."

          This work is also known as "The History of the Prophets and Kings".

          It is a significant historical chronicle of early Islamic history.

          235

          What is the compulsory tax on the products of agricultural land?

          Correct Answer: Ushr
          Show explanation
          Explanation
          • Ushr is a compulsory Islamic tax on the products of agricultural land.
          • Ushr is a 10% tax on harvests from irrigated or rain-watered land.
            • 5% on harvests from land dependent on well water.
            236

            The name of third kalimah is ______?

            Correct Answer: Kalimah-e-Tamjeed
            Show explanation
            Explanation
            The third kalima is also referred to as tamjeed.
            This is referred to as word of praise,
            word of glorification,
            or word of glory to Allah.

            237

            Name the Kalima which is recited in a funeral procession?

            Correct Answer: Shahadat
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The Kalima Shahadat, which declares the testimony of faith.

            It is recited during a funeral procession in Islamic traditions.

            238

            قرآن عظیم کس فرشتے کے ذریعے نازل ہوا تھا۔

            Correct Answer: جبرائیل
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Quran was revealed through the archangel Gabriel (Jibril)

            قرآن کا نزول 23 سالوں میں مکمل ہوا

            Quran revelation was beginning on 22 December 609 CE,
            The Quran was orally revealed when Muhammad was 40 years old

            Iqra was the first revealed word in the Holy Quran

            5 verses of Surah Al-Alaq were first revealed by the Holy Prophet (PBUH)

            Surah Alaq was the first verse in the Holy Quran revealed

            Surah Al Nasr was last revealed


            239

            Holy Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) has been mentioned as Ahmed in which Surah of the Holy Quran?

            Correct Answer: As-Saff
            Show explanation
            Explanation
            • Name “Ahmed”, one time mentioned in the whole Quran in Surah As-Saff (61:6)
            • It appears in Surah As-Saff (61:6) in the context of the prophecy of Isa (Jesus), son of Mary, about the coming of a messenger after him.

            ایسا سورہ الصف (61:6) میں عیسیٰ ابن مریم کی ایک پیغمبر کے بارے میں پیشین گوئی کے تناظر میں ظاہر ہوتا ہے جو ان کے بعد آئے گا۔

            Additional information: 

            • The name “Muhammad” is mentioned four times in the whole Quran.
              • Surah Ali-Imran: ayat 144.
              • Surah al-Ahzab: ayat 40.
              • Surah Muhammad: ayat 2.
              • Surah al-Fath: ayat 29

            Last updated by NS on 01 MAY 2026

            240

            What is ethics primarily concerned with?

            Correct Answer: Morality
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Ethics is primarily concerned with questions of morality, which involves principles of right and wrong behavior.

            241

            Which of the following is an essential component of ethical behavior?

            Correct Answer: Honesty and integrity
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Honesty and integrity are fundamental components of ethical behavior, reflecting adherence to moral principles and truthfulness.

            242

            Who was the leader of Muslim in the Battle of Badr (Jang-e-Qasiyah)?

            Correct Answer: Muhammad (PBUH)
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The leader of the Muslims in the Battle of Badr was Prophet Muhammad (PBUH).

            All important questions and information related to battle of Badr

            Badr means in Arabic: Full moon

            Furqan the second name of the battle of Badr

            Abu Jehal was the leader of the kufars in the battle of Badar.

            The battle of Badr was the first battle of Islam 

            70 infidels were killed in Ghazwa Badr

            The Battle of Badr is also referred to as The Day of the Criterion.

            It was fought on Tuesday, 13 March 624 CE (17 Ramadan, 2 AH), near the city of Badr.

            14 Muslims were killed in the Battle of Badr.

            No of the Kufars (Non-Muslims Forces) in the Battle of Badr were 1000

             mentioned in Surat Al-Anfal 

            1000 angels participated in the Battle of Badr

            Total: Muslim 313-317

            Muhajirun: 82-85

            Ansar: 231


            243

            Name the first authority for the compilation of Ahadis?

            Correct Answer: Imam Maalik
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Al-Muwatta is the first compilation of Ahadis written by Imam Malik.

            Imam Malik was Born 711 AD, Medina, Saudi Arabia.

            Died: 795 AD, Medina, Saudi Arabia.

            244

            سبت اور رفع کس نبی سے منسلک ہے؟

            Correct Answer: حضرت عیسیٰ
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            سبت اور رفع حضرت عیسیٰ علیہ السلام سے منسلک ہیں

            ۔


            245

            کس غزوہ میں مسلمانوں پر نیند طاری ہوئی؟

            Correct Answer: غزوہ بدر
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            غزوہ بدر میں، جب مسلمانوں کا مقابلہ قریش کی فوج سے ہوا، تو اللہ نے مسلمانوں پر نیند طاری کی اور بارش کے ذریعے ان کی مدد کی۔ یہ واقعہ مسلمانوں کے لیے ایک عظیم روحانی اور جسمانی سہارا تھا جس نے انہیں دشمن کے خلاف لڑنے کی قوت بخشی۔







            246

            رسالہ مجلہ قانون کس کی عدالت میں جاری ہوا؟

            Correct Answer: اسلامی عدالت
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The "رسالہ مجلہ قانون" was issued by an Islamic court, representing Islamic legal jurisprudence.


            247

            The concept of 'Istihsan' in Islamic jurisprudence is attributed to:

            Correct Answer: Imam Abu Hanifa
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Istehsan is a juristic preference used in Islamic law, associated with Imam Abu Hanifa.




            248

            "Good consists in creating the greatest happiness for the largest number of people". This theory is termed as _____?

            Correct Answer: Utilitarianism
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The theory stating that "good consists in creating the greatest happiness for the largest number of people" is known as Utilitarianism.

            Utilitarianism, a concept popularized by philosophers like Jeremy Bentham and John Stuart Mill.

            249

            Which river is often mentioned in the Bible?

            Correct Answer: Jordan
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Three rivers in the Bible are mentioned most often:

            • the Euphrates,
            • the Nile, 
            • the Jordan
            250

            According to the Islamic Principle of Inheritance, a male's share in deceased father's property viz-a-vtz female is _____?

            Correct Answer: Two times
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            According to the Islamic Principle of Inheritance, a male's share in a deceased father's property is two times that of a female.

            This means if a son receives two shares, a daughter receives one share.

            251

            "Bona fide" means _____?

            Correct Answer: Acting in good faith
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            "Bona fide" is a Latin term meaning "in good faith."

            It refers to actions or intentions that are genuine, honest, and sincere without any intention to deceive.

            252

            First Islamic summit of OIC was held in which country?

            Correct Answer: Rabat
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            1st Session of The Islamic Summit Conference Rabat, Kingdom of Morocco

            (September 1969)

            ****

            Additional information

            Islamic Summit held in Riyadh, Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, on 11 November 2023


            5th Session of The Islamic Summit Conference Kuwait, State of Kuwait (26-29 January 1987)

            4th Session of The Islamic Summit Conference Casablanca, Kingdom of Morocco (16-19 January 1984)

            3rd Session of The Islamic Summit Conference Makkah Al-Mukarramah, Saudi Arabia (25-28 January 1981)

            2nd Session of The Islamic Summit Conference Lahore,  Islamic Republic of Pakistan (22 - 24 February 1974)

            ND04-08-2023

            253

            What do you understand by ‘Al-Fay’?

            Correct Answer: Lands conquered by Muslims which became the property of Islamic state
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            'Al-Fay' refers to lands and properties that were conquered by Muslims without a direct battle and became the property of the Islamic state.

            These resources were distributed according to Islamic law for the benefit of the community.

            254

            What is the name of first Kalima?

            Correct Answer: Kalimah Tayeeba
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The name of the first Kalimah is Kalimah Tayeeba

            Seven Words are there in the First Kalimah.

            48 letters in the first Kalima.

            There is no doots in first kalima


            Additional information


            List of six Kalimas

            1

            كَلِمَاتْ اَلطَّيِّبَة‎

            (Word of Purity)

            2

            كَلِمَاتْ اَلشَّهَادَة‎

            (Word of Testimony)

            3

            كَلِمَاتْ اَلتَّمْجِيدْ‎

            (Word of Majesty)

            4

            كَلِمَاتْ اَلتَّوْحِيدْ‎

            (Word of Oneness)

            5

            كَلِمَاتْ إِسْتِغْفَارْ‎

            (Word of Penitence)

            6

            كَلِمَاتْ رَدّْ اَلْكُفْرْ‎

            ("Word of Rejection of Disbelief")

            255

            Before Damascus which city was the capital of Muslim Empire?

            Correct Answer: Kufa
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Before Damascus, the city of Kufa served as the capital of the Muslim Empire.

            This was during the rule of Caliph Hazrat Ali (RA), the fourth caliph of Islam.

            Kufa was made capital in 36 A.H.

            256

            Which of the following Surah in the Holy Quran named after insects?

            Correct Answer: Surah Al-Naml
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Surah Al-Naml (The Ant) is named after the ants mentioned in the story of Prophet Sulaiman (AS).

            It is the 27th Surah in the Quran.

            257

            On which Prophet Taurat (Torat) was revealed?

            Correct Answer: Hazrat Mussa
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            "Torah" means "Law" in Hebrew.

            The Torah has a range of meanings.

            It refers to the first five books of the Hebrew Bible.

            It is also known as the Five Books of Moses or Chameesha Choomshey Torah in Hebrew.

            It encompasses the laws and teachings given to the Israelites.

            The Torah contains 187 chapters and 5,852 verses.


            There are four Holy Books

            1. The Torat  revealed to Musa
            2. The Zabur revealed to Dawud 
            3. The Injil (Gospel) revealed to Isa 
            4. The Quran was revealed to Muhammad.
            258

            Who was the childhood friend of Hazrat Muhammad (PBUH)?

            Correct Answer: Hazrat Abu Bakr (RA)
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Hazrat Abu Bakar (R.A): One of the closest friends of Prophet Muhammad (PBUH).

            Abu Bakr was born in Mecca and was two years younger than the Prophet Muhammad (PBUH)

            His real name was Abdullah Ibn Abi Quhafah.

            259

            Which Prayer starts from twilight and ends before dawn?

            Correct Answer: Tahajud
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Tahajud is the prayer that starts from twilight (after the Isha prayer) and ends before dawn.

            It is a voluntary night prayer performed in the last third of the night.

            260

            Iraq was conquered in the period of?

            Correct Answer: Hazrat Umar (RA)
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Hazrat Umar (Umar ibn al-Khattab) was the caliph during the conquest of Iraq.

            This occurred in the early 7th century, expanding the Islamic empire significantly.

            He was second caliph.

            The caliph Hazrat Umar (RA) (634-44).

            261

            There are around _____ Gurdwares in Afghanistan ?

            Correct Answer: 150
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Reports in 2022 indicate that the remaining Sikh community in Afghanistan was estimated to be around 150

            262

            The Umayyad Caliphate was overthrown in 751 A.D by Abbasids after the ____?

            Correct Answer: Battle of Zab
            Show explanation
            Explanation
            • The Umayyad Caliphate was overthrown in 750/751 AD
            • Abbasids defeated the Umayyads in the Battle of the Zab
            • Battle of the Zab is also known as the Battle of the Great Zab River
            • Battle of the Zab (750 AD):
              • The Abbasid forces, led by Abu al-Abbas al-Saffah,
              • Defeated the army of the last Umayyad caliph, Marwan II.
            • Abbasid Revolution (747–750 AD):
              • The revolution began in 747 in Khorasan,
              • Fueled by non-Arab Muslims (Mawali) and Shia supporters against Umayyad rule.

            ****

            Last updated by NS on 01 MAY 2026

            263

            Hurrairah means?

            Correct Answer: Cat
            Show explanation
            Explanation
            • Hurrairah (هُرَيْرَة) is an Arabic word that means "cat".
              • It is also the name of a famous companion of the Islamic Prophet Muhammad, Abu Hurairah (Abu Hurairah R.A).
              • It was known for his love of cats.
              264

              Who killed Prophet's uncle Hamza bin Abdul Mutalib (RA)?

              Correct Answer: Wahshi bin Harb
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              • Abyssinian slave Wahshi ibn Harb martyred Prophet's uncle Hamza bin Abdul Mutalib (RA)
              • Hazrat Hamza Died: on 22 March 625; Mount Uhud, Medina, Arabia
              • what is the relationship of Hazrat Hamza With Hazrat Muhammad (PBUH)?
              • Answer: Uncle
              • ***
                • When did Hazrat Hamza embrace Islam?
                    • Fifth Nabvi
                    • ***
                    • Ameer Hamza (R.A) was martyred in __________ Ghazwa.
                    • Uhad
                    • ***
                      • Hamza ibn Abdul Muttalib Paternal Uncle of 
                      • Hazrat Muhammad.
                      • ***

              265

              To which Prophet the Gospel was revealed by God?

              Correct Answer: Hazrat Essa (AS)
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The Injeel was the holy book revealed by Allah to Hazrat Esa (AS).

              Injeel means good news.

              Injeel is also called Gospel.

              Gospel means truth.

              Injeel was revealed in Hebrew.

              266

              Bait-ul-Laham is the birth place of ________, situated in Jerusalem.

              Correct Answer: Hazrat Essa
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Bethlehem is a Palestinian town south of  Jerusalem

              biblical birthplace of Jesus

              Isa is known as Jesus in Christianity.

              Hazrat Isa (As)  was born miraculously to the most admired woman in Islam, Maryam (Mary), who was a virgin.

              The Injeel book was revealed on Hazrat Esa As

              Hazrat Isa (As) was Born Bethlehem

              Hazrat Isa (As) is known Rohullah

              ND18-2-2023
              267

              Hazrat _____ (R.A) has the title of "gateway to knowledge".

              Correct Answer: Ali
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Hazrat Ali has the title of "gateway to knowledge"

              Ali ibn Abi Talib was a cousin, son-in-law, and companion of the Islamic prophet Muhammad.

              He ruled as the fourth Rightly Guided caliph from 656 until his assassination in 661.

              Born: March 17, 599 AD, Mecca, Saudi Arabia

              ND5-2-2023

              268

              Which Prophet is associated with the building of the Kaaba, which is central focus of the Hajj pilgrimage?

              Correct Answer: Prophet Ibrahim (Abraham)
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Prophet Ibrahim (Abraham) is associated with the building of the Kaaba, which is the central focus of the Hajj pilgrimage.

              According to Islamic tradition, Prophet Ibrahim, along with his son Prophet Isma'il (Ishmael),

              constructed the Kaaba as the first house of worship dedicated to the worship of the One God.

              حضرت ابراہیم (ابراہیم) کا تعلق خانہ کعبہ کی عمارت سے ہے جو کہ حج کی زیارت کا مرکزی مرکز ہے۔

              اسلامی روایت کے مطابق، حضرت ابراہیم نے اپنے بیٹے حضرت اسماعیل (اسماعیل) کے ساتھ مل کر خانہ کعبہ کو پہلی عبادت گاہ کے طور پر تعمیر کیا جو ایک خدا کی عبادت کے لیے وقف تھا۔

              269

              Fajar, Zuhar, Asar, Maghrib and ______ are compulsory salat.

              Correct Answer: Isha
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              • Fajar, Zuhar, Asar, Maghrib and isha are compulsory salat.
              • ..
              • Namaz commanded in Quran for 700 times.
              • Salat made compulsory in 10th Hijri.
              • Salat Juma became Farz in Madina.
              270

              What is the literal meaning of the name Hazrat Hood (AS)?

              Correct Answer: Guide
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The literal meaning of the name Hazrat Hood (AS) is "Guide."

              Hazrat Hood (AS) was a prophet sent to guide the people of Aad towards the right path.

              271

              What is the meaning of the Muslim girl name "Nasara"?

              Correct Answer: Beautiful
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Nasara (نسارا) is a Muslim girl name that means "Beautiful" or "Blooming Flower".

              It is a lovely and delicate name that symbolizes beauty, grace, and elegance.

              272

              شرعی میں ابتداء سے کیا مراد ہے؟

              Correct Answer: کسی حکم کا آغاز
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              شرعی میں ابتداء سے مراد کسی شرعی حکم یا قانون کا آغاز ہے۔


              یہ اصطلاح اس وقت استعمال ہوتی ہے جب کسی نئے شرعی حکم کی ابتدا کی جاتی ہے


              یا کسی موجودہ حکم کی ابتدائی تشریح کی جاتی ہے۔







              273

              The Holy Prophet called the House of Peace to which house?

              Correct Answer: Kaaba
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The term "House of Peace" is used by the Holy Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) to refer to a Kaba


              Additional information 

              The structure of Kaaba 1st Built by Hazrat Ibrahim AS and his son, Ismail, constructed the Kaaba
              ***
              Abraham built al-Ka'ba and called for Hajj 5,000 years ago
              The cover of the Kaaba is called Kiswah
              Hazrat Usman bin Talha (R.A): (Key holder of Kaaba)
              Hajr-e-Aswad is installed on the wall of Kaaba, what is the meaning of Aswadblack
              Tawaf is called the seven times walks around the Kaaba
              The first and innermost circle around Kaaba is Masjid ul Haram

              274

              Hazrat Muhammad (PBUH) wrote letters to kings in which year?

              Correct Answer: 6 AH
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              In a letter sent to Egyptian ruler Al-Muqawqis in the sixth year of hijra


              4 kings accepted Islam when the Holy Prophet sent them letters

              Perumal is the first king to accept Islam at the hands of Prophet Muhammad


              275

              Hudood Ordinance was introduced in ______?

              Correct Answer: 1399 Hijri
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The Hudood Ordinance was introduced in 1399 Hijri (1979) in the.

              It was a set of laws enacted in Pakistan to enforce Islamic criminal law.

              276

              Who was the Third Caliph of Islam?

              Correct Answer: Hazrat Usman(R.A.)
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Hazrat Usman (R.A)

              Belonged

              Quraysh

              Born

              Makkah

              Caliph

              Third caliph

              Caliphate

              23 to 35 Hijjah

              Caliphate

              644 to 656 CE

              Titles

              Zun-Noorain & Jame-al-Quran

              Martyred

              18th Zul-Hijjah 35 A.H

              Martyrdom day

              Friday

              Wives

              Ruqayyah (RA) & Umm-e-Kulthum (RA)

              Full Name

              Usman bin Affaan

              Death cause

              Martyred by rebels

              Who was the First Caliph?

              Hazrat Abu Bakar was the first caliph after Holy Prophet.

              Hazrat Abu Bakar remained Chalipha 8 June 632 – 23 August 634
              ***

              Who was the second Caliph?

              Hazrat Umar(R.A.)
              ***
              Who was the Third Caliph of Islam?

               Hazrat Usman(R.A.)
              ***


              Who was the fourth Caliph?

               Hazrat Ali (R.A.)

              Hazrat Ali(R.A.) Caliph period from 656 till 661 CE

              ***

              Which sahabi proposed the name of Hazrat Abu Bakr (R.A) for the first Caliphate of Islam?

              Hazrat Umar (R.A)

              ND10-3-2023








              277

              Zakat is a _____ kind of worship.

              Correct Answer: Financial
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Zakat is a financial kind of worship.

              It is the third pillar of Islam.

              It involves giving a fixed portion of one's wealth to those in need.

              278

              The belief that no Prophet will come after Muhammad SAW is called _____?

              Correct Answer: Khatm-e-Nubuwat
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The belief that no Prophet will come after Muhammad SAW is called Khatm-e-Nubuwat.

              This concept asserts that Muhammad SAW is the final Prophet, and the line of Prophethood has concluded with him.

              279

              The Tasbeeh of Subhana Rabi-al-Azeem is recited in?

              Correct Answer: Rakuh
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The Tasbeeh of Subhana Rabi-al-Azeem is recited in Rukku.

              While the Tasbeeh of Subhana Rabi-al-Alaa is recited in Sajda.

              280

              On which time the Holy Prophet (PBUH) was gifted with Namaz?

              Correct Answer: Miraj
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Namaz commanded in Quran for 700 times.

              Salat made compulsory in 10th Nabvi.

              Salat Juma became Farz in Madina.

              281

              The uniqueness of Islamic law lies in their comprehensiveness of ______?

              Correct Answer: Principles
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Islamic law is unique due to its comprehensiveness of principles.

              It cover various aspects of life, including ethics, personal conduct, and legal matters.

              282

              For how long, was the caliphate of Hazrat Usman (RA) tested?

              Correct Answer: 12 years
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Caliph Uthman ruled for a total of twelve years between 644 and 656.

              This made him the longest reigning caliph out of the Rashidun Caliphs

              Hazrat Usman (R.A)

              Wives

              Ruqayyah (RA) & Umm-e-Kulthum (RA)

              Full Name

              Usman bin Affaan

              Death cause

              Martyred by rebels

              283

              A marriage is dissolved in case either the husband or wife becomes ______?

              Correct Answer: Deceased
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              A marriage is dissolved if either the husband or wife becomes deceased.

              This event legally terminates the marital union.

              284

              Who was the Second Wife of Hazrat Muhammad ﷺ?

              Correct Answer: Hazrat Sauda (رضہ)
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Hazrat Sauda was the 2nd wife of Hazrat Muhammad ﷺ.

              She was 50 years old at the time of Nikah and Muhammad ﷺ was also 50 years.

              Prophet Muhammad SAW married Sauda in Ramadan,  a few months after the death of Khadija.

              Additional Information:

              Nabi e Akram (ﷺ) had eleven (11) wives also known as “Azwaaj e Mutahharaat”

              285

              Which sin will not be forgiven by Allah?

              Correct Answer: Shirk
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              شرک تمام گناہوں میں سب سے گھناؤنا اور سب سے کبیرہ گناہ ہے 

              286

              World's first 3D-printed mosque has been unveiled in which country?

              Correct Answer: Saudi Arabia
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              World's First-Ever 3D Printed Mosque Opened in Saudi Arabia.

              The Kingdom of Saudi Arabia is now home to the world's first 3D-printed mosque, spanning an area of 5,600 sqm.

              World's first 3D-printed mosque has been unveiled in Jeddah in 2024.

              ****



              287

              In Holy Torat which name Prophet Muhammad (ﷺ) Mentioned:

              Correct Answer: Tayyab
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              In the Holy Torah (Torat), Prophet Muhammad (ﷺ) is referred to by the name "Ahmad"


              Torah and the Zabur were revealed in Hebrew.

              The name “Muhammad”, is four times mentioned in the whole Quran.

              1.       Surah ali-Imran: ayat 144.

              2.      Surah al-Ahzab: ayat 40.

              3.       Surah Muhammad: ayat 2.

              4.       Surah al-Fath: ayat 29.

               

              The name “Ahmed”, is one time mentioned in the whole Quran   s-Saff: Ayat 6.

              288

              Which one was in practice on the previous Prophets followers before Islam?

              Correct Answer: Salat
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Salat (prayer) was in practice among the followers of previous prophets before Islam.

              Regular prayer was a common element of worship in the monotheistic traditions of earlier prophets.

              289

              Imam Ahmad bin Hanbal was born in ______?

              Correct Answer: 164 A.H
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Imam Ahmad bin Hanbal was born in 164 A.H in Baghdad.

              He passed away in 241 A.H.

              He was a notable Islamic scholar.

              He was the founder of the Hanbali school of jurisprudence, having studied under Imam Shafi'i.

              290

              From which country did the famous companion Hazrat Sohaib bin Sinan Rumi (RA) belong?

              Correct Answer: Rome
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Hazrat Sohaib bin Sinan Rumi (RA) belonged to Rome.

              He was born around 592 CE in Mosul, Mesopotamia.

              He was died around 659 CE in Medina.

              Despite his Roman origins, he was of Arab ethnicity.

              He is buried in Jannat al-Baqi', Hejaz (modern-day Saudi Arabia).

              291

              Who performed the Nikah khutba (marriage sermon) of Hazrat Muhammad SAW and Hazrat Khadija RA?

              Correct Answer: Hazrat Abu Talib (RA)
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The Nikah khutba (marriage sermon) of Hazrat Muhammad SAW and Hazrat Khadija RA was performed by Hazrat Abu Talib (RA).

              He was the uncle of the Prophet Muhammad SAW.

              He was acted as the guardian during the marriage.

              292

              What was the name of the king of Abyssinia?

              Correct Answer: Najashi
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The king of Abyssinia (modern-day Ethiopia) was Najashi.

              He is noted for his favorable reception of early Muslim refugees during the time of the Prophet Muhammad SAW.

              293

              Name the Uncle of the Holy Prophet (PBUH) who was martyred in the battle of Uhud?

              Correct Answer: Hazrat Hamza (RA)
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Uncle of the Holy Prophet (PBUH) named Hazrat Hamza (RA) was martyred in the battle of Uhad.

              Abyssinian slave Wahshi ibn Harb martyred Hazrzat Hamza bin Abdul Mutalib (RA)

              Hazrat Hamza Died: on 22 March 625; Mount Uhud, Medina, Arabia

               

               

              Additional info

              Battle of Uhad was fought in third Hijra in the month of Shawal.

               ****

              294

              To whom Hazrat Khadija (R.A) took to the Holy Prophet (SAW) after discussing firstly about the prophethood?

              Correct Answer: Warqa bin Nofal
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Khadija (R.A) took the Holy Prophet (SAW) to Warqa bin Nofal after discussing firstly about the prophethood.

              Waraqah ibn Nawfal was the cousin Uncle of Hazrat Khadija (RA).

              Hazrat Khadija (RA) took him to Waraqah bin Naufal who was a Christian convert and used to read the Gospel in Arabic.

              Khadija (RA) was the first wife of the Holy Prophet (PBHU).


              Additional Info

              Prophethood has been finished on the Prophet Hazrat Muhammad (PBUH)

              The Prophethood is belief in All the Prophets of Allah.

              Prophethood is attained by  Bestowed by Allah

              ***


              295

              How many days Hazrat Muhammad PBUH stayed during Tabook Battle?

              Correct Answer: 20
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              • Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) stayed for 20 days in Tabuk
              • The Battle of Tabuk, the last major expedition of the Prophet Muhammad (PBUH).
              • It was fought against the Romans in 9 Hijri (630 AD).
              • It took place in the region of Tabuk during Rajab and Sha'ban.

              Last updated by NS on 06 May 2026


              296

              Which surah is called "Al-Hamd'?

              Correct Answer: Al-Fatiha
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              • Surah Al-Fatiha is also known as Al-Hamd (The Praise).
                It has 7 verses, 29 words, and 139 letters.
                It is also referred to as:
                • As-Salah (The Prayer)
                • Umm al-Kitab (Mother of the Book)
                • Umm al-Quran (Mother of the Quran)
                • Sab'a min al-Mathani (Seven Repeated Ones)
                • Ash-Shifa' (The Cure).
              297

              Hazrat Umar (RA) was Caliph for______years.

              Correct Answer: 10 years
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Hazrat Umar ibn al-Khattab was the second caliph of Islam.

              He was ruled for 10 years from 634 CE to 644 CE.

              He succeeded Hazrat Abu Bakr.

              He was instrumental in expanding the Islamic state.
              298

              اشاروں کنایوں کے الفاظ سے جو طلاق دی جائے کہلا تی ہے؟

              Correct Answer: طلاق بائن
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              اشاروں اور کنایات کے الفاظ سے دی جانے والی طلاق کو "طلاق کنائی" یا "طلاق بائن" کہا جاتا ہے۔


              طلاق بائن وہ طلاق ہے جو کسی بھی دوبارہ رجوع (تعلق بحال) کے بغیر ایک بار دی جائے۔

              طلاق کنائی میں شوہر ایسے الفاظ استعمال کرتا ہے جو براہ راست طلاق کا اعلان نہیں کرتے، بلکہ اشارہ یا کنایہ ہوتا ہے کہ تعلق ختم ہو چکا ہے۔


              طلاق بائن کی اقسام


              طلاق بائن صغری: جب شوہر پہلی یا دوسری بار طلاق دیتا ہے اور اس کے بعد عدت گزر جاتی ہے، تو یہ طلاق بائن صغری کہلاتی ہے۔


              طلاق بائن کبری: جب شوہر تیسری بار طلاق دیتا ہے، تو یہ طلاق بائن کبری کہلاتی ہے اور اس کے بعد شوہر اور بیوی کے درمیان دوبارہ نکاح ممکن نہیں ہوتا جب تک کہ بیوی کسی اور سے نکاح نہ کرے۔


              مثالیں:

              "تم آزاد ہو"

              "اب ہم الگ ہو چکے ہیں"

              "تم میرے لیے نہیں ہو"

              299

              پہلا جنگی بحری بیڑا کس نے تیار کیا تھا؟

              Correct Answer: حضرت عثمان بن عفان
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              حضرت عثمان بن عفان نے اسلامی سلطنت میں پہلا جنگی بحری بیڑا تیار کیا۔

              یہ بیڑا 29 ہجری میں تیار کیا گیا تھا اور اس کا مقصد بحری جنگی کی صلاحیت کو بہتر بنانا تھا۔


              300

              تالمود کس مزہب کی کتاب ہے؟

              Correct Answer: یہودیت
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              تالمود یہودیت کی مقدس کتاب ہے۔ یہ کتاب یسراٰئیلی (یہودی) قوانین، روایات، اور فلسفے کا مجموعہ ہے۔

              تالمود دو بنیادی حصوں پر مشتمل ہے

              میشنا: یہ حصہ ابتدائی یہودی قوانین اور احکام پر مشتمل ہے، جو پہلے صدیوں میں تحریر کیے گئے۔

              گیمارا: یہ حصہ مشنا کی تشریحات اور تبصرے پر مشتمل ہے، جو بعد کے صدیوں میں تحریر کیا گیا۔

              301

              Where the torait was revealed on Hazrat Musa (AS)?

              Correct Answer: Koh-e-Toor
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The Torah was revealed to Hazrat Musa (AS) at Koh-e-Toor (Mount Sinai).

              It comprises the five books of Musa.

              It known in Hebrew as Chameesha Choomshey Torah.

              The word 'Torait' appears 18 times in the Quran.

              Hazrat Musa's name is mentioned 136 times.

              The four Holy Books are the:

              1. Torah (Musa)
              2. Zabur (Dawud)
              3. Injil (Isa)
              4. Quran (Muhammad).
              302

              Who among the companion (Sahaba) listened to the whole of the Quran from the Prophet Muhammad (SAW)?

              Correct Answer: Abdullah bin Masud
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Abdullah bin Masud was the companion who listened to the entire Quran from Prophet Muhammad (SAW).

              He was a renowned scholar and memorizer of the Quran among the Sahaba.

              303

              The place chosen to sacrifice Hazrat Ismail (AS) was ____?

              Correct Answer: Mina
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The place chosen to sacrifice Hazrat Ismail (AS) was Mina.

              This site, near Mecca, is integral to the Hajj rituals.

              Allah (SWT) provided a ram to be sacrificed instead of Ismail (AS).

              304

              Which prophet was tilted as 'Zabeeh Ullah' (Zabiullah)?

              Correct Answer: Hazrat Ismail
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              • Hazrat Ismail (AS) is called 'Zabeeh Ullah' (Zabiullah).
                • Hazrat Ismail (A.S) was Son of Hazrat Ibrahim (A.S) & Hazrat Hajra (A.S).
                • Prophet Ismail (AS) and Prophet Ishaq (AS) are Brothers.
                305

                Aitekaaf (Seclusion) during the month of Ramzan is ____?

                Correct Answer: Sunnah
                Show explanation
                Explanation
                • Aitekaaf (seclusion) during Ramadan is considered Sunnah Muakkadah, an emphasized Sunnah.
                • While not obligatory for every Muslim.
                • It is highly recommended and carries significant spiritual benefits.
                306

                Which article of the constitution of 1973 is related to the affairs of Zakat?

                Correct Answer: 31
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                Article 31 of the 1973 Constitution of Pakistan deals with Zakat.

                It establishing a system to support the welfare of the needy.

                307

                Jizya means _____?

                Correct Answer: Poll tax on non Muslims
                Show explanation
                Explanation
                • Jizya is a poll tax historically imposed on non-Muslims in Muslim states.
                  • In India, Qutb-ud-din Aibak was the first to impose Jizya.
                  • The tax was abolished by Mughal ruler Akbar in the 16th century but reintroduced by Aurangzeb in the 17th century.
                  • Kharaj was a land tax imposed on non-Muslim cultivators and landlords.
                  308

                  المساکین کس ام المومنین کا لقب ہے ؟

                  Correct Answer: حضرت زینب بنت خزیمہ رضی اللہ عنہا
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  ام المساکین حضرت زینب بنت خزیمہ رضی اللہ عنہا کا لقب ہے۔

                  ان کا یہ لقب ان کی سخاوت اور غریبوں اور مسکینوں کی مدد کی وجہ سے مشہور ہوا

                  حضرت زینب بنت خزیمہ رضی اللہ عنہا کو "ام المساکین" کا لقب ان کے زمانے کے لوگوں نے دیا تھا۔

                  309

                  On 1st Shawwal, 8th Hijra a strong Islamic force approached the valley of Hunain?

                  Correct Answer: 12000
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  On 1st Shawwal, 8th Hijra (630 CE), a force of 12,000 Muslims approached the valley of Hunain.

                  The Battle of Hunain, fought near al-Ta'if.

                  The Battle of Hunain resulted in a Muslim victory over the Bedouin tribes.
                  310

                  The nation of _______ was exterminated through the scourge of floods.

                  Correct Answer: Hazrat Nooh (A.S)
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The nation of Hazrat Nooh (A.S) was exterminated through the scourge of floods.

                  The flood is also known as Tofaan-e-Noah.

                  It was a divine punishment for their disbelief and corruption.

                  The duration of the Toofan-e-Noah (Flood of Noah) was around 6 months.

                  The event occurred 2,242 years after Hazrat Adam (AS).

                  About 80 people were with him in the boat.

                  The boat of Hazrat Noah (A.S) is referred to as the Ark.

                  Hazrat Noah (A.S) and those with him remained in the Ark for about six months.

                  After the floodwaters receded, the Ark is believed to have come to rest on Mount Judi in Turkey.

                  The nation of Noah worshiped five idols:

                  1. Wadh
                  2. Suwa
                  3. Yaghuth
                  4. Yauq
                  5. Nas

                  Hazrat Noah (AS) lived for 950 years.

                  He was sent to Iraq.

                  He is called the predecessor, Naji Ullah, and Shaikh ul Ambiya.

                  His title is Abu ul Bashr Sani.

                  After the flood, the city of Khasran was established.

                  311

                  Adam is a word of which language?

                  Correct Answer: Hebrew
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The height of Hazrat Adam (AS) was 90 feet.

                  Adam is a word of Herbew language.

                  Hebrew is official language in Israel.

                  He was the first Muslim, as the Quran states that all the Prophets preached the same faith of Islam.

                  Known for being the first human being.

                  His spouse was Hawwā (حواء).

                  Hazrat Adam (AS) was 130 years old when Shees (AS) was born.

                  He spoke the language in the Garden of Eden.

                  His total age was 930 years.

                  Hazrat Adam (AS) met with the Holy Prophet ﷺ on the 1st heaven.

                  He had 3 sons.

                  His grave is located in Sri Lanka.

                  312

                  The Grand Mosque at Damascus is located in ____?

                  Correct Answer: Syria
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The Great Mosque of Damascus is also known as the Umayyad Mosque.

                  It was built by the Umayyad Caliph al-Walīd I between 705 and 715 CE.

                  It is located in the old city of Damascus.

                  It is the capital of Syria.

                  It is one of the largest and oldest mosques in the world.

                  The mosque opened in 715 AD.

                  313

                  Which one out of the following is a renowned biographer of the Holy Prophet (PBUH)?

                  Correct Answer: Micherl H Hart
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation
                  • Michael H. Hart, renowned for his biography of the Holy Prophet (PBUH).
                  • He authored "The 100: A Ranking of the Most Influential Persons in History," first published in 1978.
                  • In this book, Muhammad (PBUH) was ranked as the most influential person in history, ahead of Jesus or Moses.
                  • The book has two editions: the first in 1978 and the second in 1992.


                  314

                  How many vowels does Arabic Language have?

                  Correct Answer: 3
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The Arabic language has three vowels: /a/, /i/, and /u/.

                  These vowels, which can be short or long, affect the pronunciation and meaning of words.

                  Vowels were inserted in the Quran in 43 Hijri.
                  315

                  Fulfillment of valid needs causes ____?
