                  Correct Answer: Satisfaction
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  "Fulfillment of valid needs causes satisfaction."

                  Meeting genuine needs leads to a sense of contentment and well-being.

                  316

                  You are in the parking lot of a grocery store. You notice a couple loading their groceries into their car. You see them getting into their car, leaving a bottle of 10 liters of cooking oil on the bottom of the trolley. What will you do?

                  Correct Answer: Approach the car before they pull away and tell them that they left their oil in the trolley
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  "Approach the car before they pull away and tell them that they left their oil in the trolley" is the best action.

                  This response is ethical and considerate.

                  It ensuring the couple retrieves their forgotten item.

                  317

                  Which of the following action depicts the "keeping of principle"?

                  Correct Answer: Doing such action which is ethically necessary to do
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  "Doing such action which is ethically necessary to do" depicts the "keeping of principle."

                  This means adhering to ethical standards and principles even without external enforcement or supervision.

                  318

                  Laws of nature are different from laws of ethics in which regard?

                  Correct Answer: Laws of nature cannot be broken
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  "Laws of nature cannot be broken" distinguishes them from laws of ethics.

                  Natural laws describe consistent, unbreakable phenomena in the physical world.

                  Whereas ethical laws can be violated by human actions.

                  319

                  How can one become a good human being?

                  Correct Answer: By following God's order
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  "By following God's order" is how one can become a good human being.

                  Aligning actions with divine guidance often encourages moral and ethical behavior.

                  It contributing to personal and communal well-being.

                  320

                  What is one's good ethical character?

                  Correct Answer: To do good because it's a right action
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  "To do good because it's a right action" reflects one's good ethical character.

                  Acting rightly based on moral principles.

                  321

                  All regions resemble resembles in which basic point?

                  Correct Answer: Oneness of God
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  All regions and religions resemble each other in the belief in the Oneness of God.

                  This fundamental concept transcends geographical and cultural boundaries.

                  It emphasizing the unity and singularity of the divine.

                  322

                  Which of the following sajdah is performed at the end of a prayer if a mistake is made while performing a prayer?

                  Correct Answer: Sajda-e-Sahw
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  "Sajda-e-Sahw" is the sajdah performed at the end of a prayer if a mistake is made while performing a prayer.

                  This prostration is done to compensate for errors or omissions in the prayer.

                  323

                  What is the standard of respect in Islam?

                  Correct Answer: Piety
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The standard of respect in Islam is piety.

                  In Islam, respect and honor are based on an individual's piety and righteousness.

                  324

                  The best way of living is ____?

                  Correct Answer: Moderation
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Moderation suggests a balanced and reasonable approach to life.

                  It avoiding extremes and excesses.

                  325

                  What is called that place where Hazrat Ibrahim (AS) stood while constructing the Kaaba?

                  Correct Answer: Maqam-e-Ibrahim
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation
                  • Maqam Ibrahim is the place where Hazrat Ibrahim (AS) stood while constructing the Kaaba.
                  • It is located near the Kaaba in Makkah.
                  • It is sacred and associated with the footprint of Prophet Ibrahim (AS).
                  326

                  Who was the commanders of the Infidels in the Battle of Uhud?

                  Correct Answer: Abu Sufyan
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation
                  • The Battle of Uhud occurred on Saturday, 23 March 625 AD (3 Hijri) in the valley north of Mount Uhud.
                    • Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) led around 1000 Muslims in the Battle of Uhud.
                    • They faced 3000 Qurayshi Meccans commanded by Abu Sufyan ibn Harb.
                    • Abdullah bin Jubair was appointed by Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) to lead the 50 skilled archers positioned on Uhud Mountain.
                    327

                    Abu-al-Hikam is the title of?

                    Correct Answer: Abu Jahal
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    • Abu Jahal, commonly known as Abu Hakam (“Father of Wisdom”) among the Quraysh, was considered a wise man.
                      • He was fatally wounded in the Battle of Badr by Mu'adh ibn 'Afrā' and Mu'ādh ibn 'Amr.
                      • He was finally killed by Abdullah ibn Masud.
                      • He was born in 570 A.D.
                      • Abu Jahal died by beheading at the Battle of Badr.
                      • He was one of the Meccan polytheist pagan leaders from the Quraysh.
                      328

                      When the first migration to Abyssinia took place?

                      Correct Answer: 613 AD
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Surah Anfal mentions the migration of the Holy Prophet SAW.

                      The first migration to Abyssinia (Habshah) took place in 613 A.D.

                      Including Prophet Muhammad's relatives Usman Ghani (R.A) and Ruqaiyah (R.A).

                      This group comprised 12 men and 4 women.

                      The second migration to Abyssinia occurred in 616 A.D.

                      This group comprised 83 men and 18 to 19 women.

                      329

                      The comb was invented by Prophet?

                      Correct Answer: Ibrahim (AS)
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Hazrat Ibrahim (AS) is a prominent example of faith in Judaism, Christianity, and Islam.

                      He is also credited with inventing the comb.
                      330

                      What was the name of town in Israel which was the home of Hazrat Issa (AS) in his youth?

                      Correct Answer: Nazareth
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation
                      • The town in Israel that was the home of Hazrat Issa (AS) in his youth is Nazareth.
                        • It is known as the place where Jesus (AS) grew up.
                        331

                        Hazrat Abu Bakr (R.A) belonged to the tribe of _____?

                        Correct Answer: Banu Taym
                        Show explanation
                        Explanation
                        • Hazrat Abu Bakr (RA) was born on 27 October 573 CE in Mecca to a wealthy family of the Banu Taym tribe of the Quraysh.
                          • His father, Uthman, was given the laqab Abu Quhafa, and his mother, Salma bint Sakhar, was given the laqab Umm ul-Khair.
                          • He had sons named Abdullah ibn Abi Bakr and Abdul-Rah.
                          332

                          Which Sahabi was known as Brain of Arab?

                          Correct Answer: Hazrat Amr bin Al-Aas
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation
                          • The Holy Prophet (PBUH) gave the title Brain of Arab to Hazrat Amr bin al-Aas R.A.
                          • Hazrat Amr bin Al-Aas was the Arab commander.
                          • He led the Muslim conquest of Egypt.
                          • He served as Egypt's governor in 640–646 and 658–664.
                          • Hazrat Amr bin Al-Aas embraced Islam in 629 AD.
                          • He was Born: 573 AD Mecca, Hejaz.
                          • He was Died: 664 AD (aged 90–91) Egypt, Umayyad Caliphate.
                          333

                          During Which prayer, the change of Qibla happened?

                          Correct Answer: Zuhr
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          The change of Qibla from Jerusalem to the Kaaba in Mecca occurred during the Zuhr (midday) prayer in 624 CE.

                          This significant event took place in the second (2nd) year of the Hijra.
                          334

                          Bird Hudhud (Woodpecker) is mentioned in which Surah?

                          Correct Answer: Surah Namal
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation


                          The bird Hudhud (hoopoe) appears twice in Surah An-Naml (27th chapter) of the Quran.

                          It played a crucial role in carrying messages between Prophet Sulayman (AS) and the Queen of Sheba.
                          335

                          How many Umahaat ul Momineen were Hufaaz (memorizers of the Quran)?

                          Correct Answer: 3
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Three Umahaat ul Momineen (Mothers of the Believers) were Hufaaz (memorizers of the Quran):

                          1. Hazrat Aisha (RA)
                          2. Hazrat Hafsa (RA)
                          3. Hazrat Umm Salama (RA)

                          They were known for their deep knowledge and preservation of the Quranic verses.

                          336

                          Qaroon was a treasurer, he was in the reign of which Prophet?

                          Correct Answer: Musa (AS)
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Qaroon was a treasurer during the reign of Prophet Musa (AS).

                          He is often remembered for his immense wealth and arrogance.

                          This ultimately led to his downfall as recounted in the Quran.
                          337

                          In which Islamic Battle Angeles helped the Muslims?

                          Correct Answer: Badar
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation
                          • The Battle of Badr is also known as The Day of the Criterion.
                            • Day of the Criterion means کسوٹی کا دن
                            • It took place on Tuesday, 13 March 624 CE (17 Ramadan, 2 AH) near the city of Badr.
                            • Prophet Muhammad SAW, commanding his Sahaba, defeated the Quraysh army led by Abu Jahl (Amr ibn Hishām).
                            • The Kufar forces numbered 1,000. Fourteen Muslims were killed, while 70 Kufar were killed and 70 captured.
                            • Surah Al-Anfal mentions that Allah promised to reinforce the Muslims with 1,000 angels.
                            338

                            Which Surah of the following Quran is known as "Surah of Patience"?

                            Correct Answer: Surah Yusuf
                            Show explanation
                            Explanation

                            Surah Yusuf is known as the "Surah of Patience" in the Quran.

                            It narrates the story of Prophet Joseph (Yusuf).

                            It highlighting patience as a central theme throughout his trials and tribulations.

                            339

                            Which Pious Caliph had to fight against those who refuse to pay zakat?

                            Correct Answer: Hazrat Abu Bakr (RA)
                            Show explanation
                            Explanation

                            • Hazrat Abu Bakr, the first Caliph, engaged in Jihad over the issue of Zakat.
                            • During the Ridda Wars in July 632, he dispatched envoys to rebellious tribes demanding payment of Zakat, but they refused.
                            Name (in Arabic)Born (CE)Reigned from (CE)DiedRelationship with MuhammadHouse
                            Abu Bakr (أبو بكر الصديق)5738 June 63222 August 634Father of Aisha,  Muhammad's wifeBanu Taim

                            340

                            Torat (Torah) means ________?

                            Correct Answer: Law
                            Show explanation
                            Explanation

                            "Torah" means "Law" in Hebrew.

                            The Torah has a range of meanings.

                            It refers to the first five books of the Hebrew Bible.

                            It is also known as the Five Books of Moses or Chameesha Choomshey Torah in Hebrew.

                            It encompasses the laws and teachings given to the Israelites.

                            The Torah contains 187 chapters and 5,852 verses.
                            341

                            Where is the grave of Hazrat Noah (AS)?

                            Correct Answer: Jordan
                            Show explanation
                            Explanation

                            Grave of Hazrat Noah (AS) is situated in Jordan.

                            Hazrat Noah saved life at the mythic enormous flood on the earth.
                            342

                            No of Kufars (Non Muslims Forces) in Battle of Badr were _______?

                            Correct Answer: 1000
                            Show explanation
                            Explanation
                            • "Badr" means "full moon" in Arabic.
                            • The Battle of Badr, also called Furqan.
                            • The Battle of Badr is also known as The Day of the Criterion.
                            • Day of the Criterion means کسوٹی کا دن
                            • It took place on Tuesday, 13 March 624 CE (17 Ramadan, 2 AH) near the city of Badr.
                            • Prophet Muhammad SAW, commanding his Sahaba, defeated the Quraysh army led by Abu Jahl (Amr ibn Hishām).
                            • The Kufar forces numbered 1,000.
                            • It was the first battle of Islam, with 313-317 Muslims.
                            • The Muslim forces included 82-85 Muhajirun and 231 Ansar (61 from Aws and 170 from Khazraj).
                            • They had 2 horses and 70 camels.
                            • Fourteen Muslims were killed, while 70 Kufar were killed and 70 captured.
                            • Surah Al-Anfal mentions that Allah promised to reinforce the Muslims with 1,000 angels.
                            343

                            How many disciples of Hazrat Essa (AS) assisted him in preaching?

                            Correct Answer: 12
                            Show explanation
                            Explanation

                            Twelve companions assisted Hazrat Isa (AS).

                            He was born in Bethlehem.

                            He is known as Rohullah.

                            The Injeel was revealed to him.

                            344

                            In which Surah of the Holy Quran, the regulations for prisoners of war mentioned?

                            Correct Answer: Surah Nisa
                            Show explanation
                            Explanation

                            • Surah An-Nisa (Chapter 4) of the Holy Quran contains regulations for various aspects of life, including the treatment of prisoners of war (Verse 4:76).
                            • It emphasizes treating prisoners with kindness and respect, and provides guidance on their release and ransom.
                            • This Surah also focuses on social justice, women's rights, and compassion.
                            • Quran 76:8 supports the position that prisoners must be fed in a dignified manner and not forced to beg.
                            345

                            When did Ottoman Sultan Muhammad II Fateh capture the Roman Empire Capital, Constantinople?

                            Correct Answer: 1453
                            Show explanation
                            Explanation

                            Constantinople قسطنطنیہ

                            Ottoman Sultan Muhammad II Fateh captured Constantinople on May 29, 1453.

                            This marking the end of the Byzantine Empire.

                            It establishing Ottoman control over the city.
                            346

                            Fatwa Kazi Khan is the authentic fatwa of which sect?

                            Correct Answer: Fiqah Hanfia
                            Show explanation
                            Explanation

                            "Fatwa Kazi Khan" is an authoritative fatwa collection of the Fiqah Hanfia (Hanafi) sect.

                            The Fatwa-e-Qazi Khan consists of two volumes.

                            It containing 500 verses from the Holy Qur'an and 290 Traditions of the Holy Prophet SAW.

                            It covering marriage, dower, divorce, legitimacy, and guardianship of minors.
                            347

                            Which was the first institution of Islam?

                            Correct Answer: Suffah
                            Show explanation
                            Explanation

                            The Suffah was originally in the northeast corner of the Mosque.

                            When Muhammad SAW was ordered by Allah to change the Qibla towards Mecca in the south.

                            The Suffah was left at the rear of the mosque, where it remained.
                            348

                            In Masjid-e-Aqsa the word Aqsa means _____?

                            Correct Answer: Far away
                            Show explanation
                            Explanation
                            • In Masjid-e-Aqsa, the word Aqsa means "Far away" or "the remote".
                            • Hazrat Ibrahim (A.S) first laid the foundation.
                            • Al-Masjid Al-Aqsa was the first qibla in Islam.
                            • It was built 40 years after Al-Masjid Al-Haram in Makkah.

                            Last updated question by NA on 07 May 2026
                            349

                            What is the meaning of Quranic Surah Al-Nas?

                            Correct Answer: The men
                            Show explanation
                            Explanation

                            The last chapter (sūrah) of the Quran.

                            It is named "An-Nas," which means "people" or "mankind."
                            350

                            Name the Surah in Ahl-E-Kitab and Munafqeen are mentioned.

                            Correct Answer: surah munafqun
                            Show explanation
                            Explanation
                            •  In Makki Ahl-E-Kitab and Munafqeen are mentioned
                            • ***
                            • Ahl-E-Kitab and Munafqeen are mentioned in Surah Munafqun
                            • Surah Munafiqun is the 63 Surah of the Quran and consists of 11 verses.
                            • **
                            • Christians and Jews are referred to as Ahl al-Kitab,
                            • Jesus alone is mentioned in the Qur'an more than 30 times.
                            351

                            How many Rukus are there in Surah Al-Baqrah?

                            Correct Answer: 40
                            Show explanation
                            Explanation
                            • Al-Baqarah (The Cow or The Heifer) is the second and longest chapter of the Quran, with 286 verses.
                            • It derives its name from the story of the cow (vv. 67-73).
                            • Verse 282, the longest verse, is known as 'āyatu d-dayn.
                            • It consists of 25,500 letters and is positioned in Juzʼ 1–3.
                            • The chapter has 40 Rukus and is classified as Medinan.
                            • Hazrat Ibrahim (علیہ السلام) is mentioned 13 times.
                            • The verse 183 describes the obligation of fasting (soam).
                            352

                            In which Surah of Quran mentioned that the number of months are Twelve?

                            Correct Answer: Al-Tauba
                            Show explanation
                            Explanation

                            Surah Al-Tauba (Surah 9) mentions in verse 36 that "Verily, the number of months with Allah is twelve months."

                            This verse confirms the fixed lunar calendar in Islam.
                            353

                            Who was the first writer of “Wahi” in Quraish?

                            Correct Answer: Hazrat Zaid bin Sabit (RA)
                            Show explanation
                            Explanation
                            • Hazrat Zaid bin Sabit (RA) was the first to record "Wahi" among the Quraish.
                              • Hazrat Abu Bakar (R.A) appointed Zaid bin Sabit to compile the Holy Quran at the request of Hazrat Umar (R.A).
                              • Sharjeel-ibn-Hasana Kandi was the first to transcribe revelations in Makkah.
                              • The last revelation was written by Abi Ibn Kaaf.
                              • Prophet Idrees (عليه السلام) was the first to write with a pen.
                              354

                              The biggest Islamic country by area is _____?

                              Correct Answer: Kazakhstan
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              Largest Muslim country by area is Kazakhstan .

                              Largest Muslim country by Population is Indonesia.

                              ***

                              World Largest country by area is Russia.

                              World Largest Country by Population is India.

                              355

                              Amir Muawiya transferred his capital form Kufa to _____?

                              Correct Answer: Damascus
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              Amir Muawiya transferred his capital from Kufa to Damascus

                              Mu'awiya I  was the founder and first caliph of the Umayyad Caliphate serving from 661 until his death.

                               He became caliph less than thirty years after the death of the Islamic prophet Muhammad

                              Muawiyah was appointed as the Governor of Syria after his brother Yazid ibn Abu Sufyan died

                              Ameer Muawiyah (R.A) established Postal System

                              Hazrat Abu Sufyan, Hazrat Ameer Muawiyah, and Abu Kahafa (R.A) accepted Islam at the time of the Conquest of Makkah

                              ****

                              nd9-2-2023


                              RY30012023


                              356

                              When Hazrat Usman (RA) accepted Islam, what was his age?

                              Correct Answer: 34 years
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              Hazrat Usman (RA), also known as Uthman ibn Affan.

                              He accepted Islam at the age of 34, in the year 616 CE.

                              He went on to become the third Rashidun Caliph of Islam.

                              357

                              Medina was a center of which of the following?

                              Correct Answer: Islamic learning and knowledge
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              Medina is generally considered to be the "cradle of Islamic culture and civilization".


                              358

                              Which member of Ashra Mubashra was the son of Hazrat Safia Bint-e-Abdul Mutalib (R.A.)?

                              Correct Answer: Hazrat Zubair (R.A)
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation
                              • Hazrat Zubair (R.A) was the son of Hazrat Safia bint Abdul Muttalib (R.A.), making him the nephew of the Prophet Muhammad (P.B.U.H.).
                              • He was one of the prominent companions of the Prophet SAW and a member of the Ashra Mubashra, the ten companions who were promised Paradise.
                              359

                              Al-Jaleel is one of the names of Allah, and it means?

                              Correct Answer: The Majestic
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              Al-Jaleel is one of the 99 names of Allah in Islam.

                              It means "The Majestic" or "The Glorious"you.

                              It is used to describe Allah's greatness and majesty.

                              360

                              In which Para (Chapter) of the Holy Quran is the verse "Wa Iza Samiu" found?

                              Correct Answer: Para 7
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation
                              • "Wa Iza Samiu" is a verse in the 7th Para (Chapter) of the Holy Quran.
                              • Specifically in Surah Al-A'raf (Chapter 7), Verse 175.
                              361

                              What does the Arabic title "Al-Hakeem" mean?

                              Correct Answer: The Wise
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              "Al-Hakeem" is one of the names of Allah in Islam.

                              It means "The Wise" or "The All-Wise".

                              362

                              First Migration to Habsha was in _______ Nabvi.

                              Correct Answer: 4th
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation
                              The First Migration to Abyssinia (Ethiopia) 613 CE (4th Nabvi)
                              Participants: A group of twelve men and four women
                              The relatives of Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) in the first migration to Abyssinia were Usman Ghani (R.A) and Ruqaiyah (R.A)
                              Also known as:Hijrah il-al-Habshah 

                              ***Additional Information***
                              The 1st Migration to Abyssinia (Ethiopia) 613 CE
                              The 2nd Migration to Abyssinia (Ethiopia) 616 CE
                              The Ethiopian Empire also formerly known by the exonym Abyssinia
                              363

                              The word "deen" is mentioned how many times in the Holy Quran?

                              Correct Answer: 79 times
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              The word "deen" (دين) is mentioned 79 times in the Quran.

                              It is an Arabic term that encompasses various meanings, including religion, faith, law, judgment, way, and obedience.

                              364

                              What does "Yum-ul-Talaq" mean?

                              Correct Answer: Day of Gathering
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              "Yum-ul-Talaq" means the Day of Gathering.

                              It refers to the day when all humans will be gathered for judgment, emphasizing the event of collective assembly before God.

                              365

                              After which Companions did the Caliphate end?

                              Correct Answer: Hazrat Ali (RA)
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              The Rashidun Caliphate ended with the martyrdom of Hazrat Ali (RA), the fourth caliph, in 661 CE.

                              He was the last of the rightly guided caliphs.

                              After his death marked the beginning of the Umayyad Caliphate, which was established by Muawiyah.

                              366

                              Those who have hoarded gold and silver and do not spend it in the way of God, give them the good news of a painful punishment. This is the translation of the verse of which Holy Quran?

                              Correct Answer: Surah Tauba
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              This verse (Surah Tauba, Verse 34).

                              It warns those who hoard wealth and fail to spend it in the way of God, promising them a painful punishment in the hereafter.

                              It emphasizes the importance of charity and generosity in Islam.

                              367

                              How many Angels are mentioned in Holy Quran?

                              Correct Answer: 7
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              There are 7 Angels are mentioned in Holy Quran.

                              1. Gabriel (upon whom be peace):
                              Gabriel is the angel whose task is to convey the revelation from Allah to His Messengers.

                              2. Mikaa’eel (upon whom be peace):
                              Mikaa’eel is the angel responsible for distribution of sustenance wherever Allah wishes.

                              3. Israafeel (upon whom be peace):
                              Israfeel (a.s.) is the angel who is charged with blowing the trumpet on the Day of Judgment.

                              4. Azraeel (upon whom be peace):
                              He is the Angel of death to take the souls with the order of Allah swt. He is mentioned in the hadiths and ayahs as “Angel of Death.”

                              5. Kiram al Katibin:
                              “Keepers” mentioned in the ayahs (Surah al Infitar; 10-12) and (Surah al An’am, 61) are the “kiram al katibin” angels that are the two angels recording the deeds.

                              6. Hafaza (The Guardian Angels)
                              “Witnesses” are the hafaza angels who are charged with guarding.

                              7. Hamalat al-‘Arsh:
                              They are the angels carrying the arsh (throne).

                              368

                              In Which city does Pakistan run an electric public bus?

                              Correct Answer: Karachi
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              Sindh Information Minister Sharjeel Inam Memon on Friday announced the launch of Pakistan's first electric bus service in Karachi.

                              The bus service is being started from Tank Chowk near Jinnah Avenue

                              The project will begin with 10 bus stops.


                              ND10-3-2023

                              369

                              Which surah is the preface of the Holy Quran?

                              Correct Answer: Surah Al-Fatiha
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              Surah Al-Fatihasurah is the preface of the Holy Quran

                              ***

                              Preface  ديباچہ, تمھيد, مقدمہ

                              Surah Al-Fatiha, the first chapter of the Quran, is recited in every unit of prayer. It consists of 7 ayah

                              • Surah of The Holy Quran meaning.
                              • Al-Fatihah (the Opening) ·
                              • Al-Baqarah (the Cow) 
                              • Aali Imran (the Family of Imran)
                              • An-Nisa' (the Women) 
                              •  Al-Ma'idah (the Table) 
                              • ***

                              ***

                              AGM 02.05.2023

                              ND23-6-2023



                              370

                              How many Surahs of the Holy Quran start with the word ‘Inna‘?

                              Correct Answer: Four
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              Holy Qur'an, certainly words from Allah (swt) contains four Surah/Chapter which starts with the word 'Inna' and chapters are the following:
                               1) Fath a.
                              2) Nuh.
                              3) Qad'r.
                              4) Kausar.
                              371

                              After how many months of migration of Prophet (PBUH) to Madina the change of Qibla occurred?

                              Correct Answer: 18
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              The change of Qibla occurred after the 18 months of migration of the Holy Prophet (PBUH) to Madina

                               the change of Qibla occurred In 624 AD 

                              During the Zohar prayer, the change of Qibla happened.

                              In 2 Hijri years, Qibla has changed

                              Masjid al-Qiblatain is one of the three oldest mosques in Islamic history

                              The first Qibla Muslims prayed for is  Al-Aqsa mosque المسجد الأقصى (Bait Al-Maqdis بيت المقدس)

                              ***


                              372

                              the last caliph of Ummayyads was:

                              Correct Answer: marwan ll
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              Marwan II  was the last of the Umayyad caliphs (reigned 744–750)

                              Marwan ibn Muhammad ibn Marwan known as Marwan II

                              He was the fourteenth and last caliph of the Umayyad Caliphate

                              He was killed while fleeing the forces of Abu al-Abbas as-Saffaḥ

                              373

                              Maqam-e-Ibrahim is the place which contains the imprints of the feet of Hazrat Ibrahim (AS). Where it is located?

                              Correct Answer: Makkah
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation
                              • Maqam-e-Ibrahim is located in Makkah, Saudi Arabia, in front of the Kaaba.
                              • The stone is encased in a glass and metal enclosure.
                              • It is situated in the Mataf area where pilgrims perform Tawaf.
                              • It is about 13 meters (43 feet) east of the Kaaba.
                              374

                              What was the name of Sword of Hazrat Muhammad SAW?

                              Correct Answer: Both A and B
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              Hazrat Muhammad (SAW) had several swords, but the most famous ones were:

                              • Azb (أزب): Also known as Azab, it was a sword given to him by his cousin, Ali ibn Abi Talib (RA).
                              • Zulfiqar (ذو الفقار): This was a sword given to him by Allah (SWT) through the angel Jibril (AS) and was considered a divine gift. It was also used by Ali ibn Abi Talib (RA) in the Battle of the Trench.
                              375

                              Mutfqa Alea is known as _____?

                              Correct Answer: Bukhari
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation
                              • Bukhari is a famous Islamic scholar who compiled the hadith collection known as Sahih al-Bukhari.
                              • It considered one of the most authentic and reliable sources of hadith in Islam.
                              • He is often referred to as Mutfqa Alea, which means "the agreed upon" or "the accepted".
                              376

                              Hazrat Imam Hussain's (RA) title was _____?

                              Correct Answer: Shabbir
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation
                              • Hazrat Imam Hussain (RA), the grandson of Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) and son of Hazrat Ali (RA) and Hazrat Fatimah (RA).
                              • He is often referred to by the title "Shabbir," which means "patient" or "enduring."
                              377

                              Hijra (migration) from Makkah to Madina started in which Nabvi year?

                              Correct Answer: 13th
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation
                              • The Hijra, or migration of Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) and his followers from Makkah to Madina.
                              • It took place in the 13th year of the Prophethood.
                              • It marking the beginning of the Islamic calendar.
                              378

                              Which Holy Book is called the New Testament?

                              Correct Answer: The Injeel
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation
                              • The New Testament is the second half of the Christian Bible.
                              • Christians consider the Old Testament (or Hebrew Bible/Jewish Scriptures)
                              • The New Testament is the sacred canon of Scripture.
                              379

                              Prophet Muhammad Attributes as Rahmatan for:

                              Correct Answer: Mercy of World
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation
                              - إِنَّ فِي هَذَا لَبَلَاغًا لِّقَوْمٍ عَابِدِينَ   
                               - وَمَا أَرْسَلْنَاكَ إِلَّا رَحْمَةً لِّلْعَالَمِينَ
                              "Indeed, in this [Quran] is notification for a worshipping people. And We have not sent you, [O Muhammad], except as a mercy to the worlds." (Quran: 21:106-107)
                              محمد ﷺ تمام جہانوں کے لیے رحمت بن کر آئے

                              380

                              Father of Hazrat Ishaq (AS) was _____?

                              Correct Answer: Hazrat Ibrahim (AS)
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              Hazrat Ishaq (Isaac) was the son of Hazrat Ibrahim (Abraham) and his wife Sarah.

                              381

                              He who does not keep a promise has no _____

                              Correct Answer: Faith
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              Hadith on Trust: No faith for one who cannot keep a promise.

                              382

                              Islamic democracy is based on

                              Correct Answer: All
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              The principles of democracy, freedom, equality, tolerance and social justice, as enunciated by Islam, shall be fully observed

                              383

                              The meaning of word RISALAT is________?

                              Correct Answer: Messenger
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              Risalat  Literally, Risalat derived from the word Rasal which means to send.

                              In Islamic terms, it means to convey the message of God to his worshippers.



                              AGM  5.2.23
                              384

                              In which surah is Muhammad name mentioned?

                              Correct Answer: All of these
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              Muhammad is the 47th surah of the Quran with 38 verses 

                              The name "Muhammad" is mentioned four times in the Quran, and the name "Ahmad"  is mentioned one time.

                              ************

                              Name “Muhammad”, four times mentioned in the whole Quran.

                              1. Aal Imraan 3: 144;
                              2. Al-Ahzaab 33: 40;
                              3. Muhammad 47: 2; and
                              4. Al-Fatah 48: 29

                              Name “Ahmed”, one time mentioned in the whole Quran.

                              1. Al-Saff 61: 6.
                              385

                              First time In which language Quran was translated

                              Correct Answer: Persian
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation
                              Quran first translated into Persian in the 18th century by Shah Wali Ullah


                              The Holy Quran was first time translated into Urdu: by Shah Abdul Qadir and Shah Rafiuddin (Mozah Al-Quran)

                              The Holy Quran was first time translated into Punjabi: Tauqir Amaan Khan

                              The Holy Quran was first time translated into Sindhi: Aakhund Azzizullah

                              The Holy Quran first time translated into English: George Sale

                              Holy Quran was translated for the first time in India Sindhi

                              Holy Quran was translated for the first time in World Persian

                              ND2023-2-12


                              386

                              Who ruled Ottoman Turkey in the 16th century?

                              Correct Answer: Suleiman
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              In the 16th century, Ottoman Turkey was primarily ruled by Suleiman the Magnificent, reigning from 1520 to 1566.

                              He was succeeded by his son Selim II, who ruled from 1566 to 1574, continuing his father's policies.

                              During the 16th and 17th centuries, the Ottoman Empire was controlling much of Southeast Europe, Western Asia, the Caucasus, North Africa, and the Horn of Africa.

                              387

                              Who is the Father of Hazrat Sheesh?

                              Correct Answer: Hazrat Adam (A.S)
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation
                              • Hazrat Adam (A.S)  is the Father of Hazrat Sheesh (A.S)
                              • The age of Hazrat Adam (A.S) at Sheesh’s (A.S) birth was 130 years
                              • Prophet Sheesh Alaihissalam was the third son of Prophet Adam Alaihissalam.
                              • 50 Sahifas were revealed to Hazrat Sheesh (Alayhis-Salaam).
                              • 10 commandments did Prophet Shees send down
                              388

                              Who died during the 'Year of Grief'?

                              Correct Answer: Both A & C
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              The Year of Sadness Year of sorrow  (عام الحزن) is the Hijri era when Prophet Hazrat Muhammad's wife Hazrat Khadija (RA) and her uncle and guardian Abu Talib (RA) died.

                               This year almost coincided with 619 AD, the tenth year since the first revelation of Muhammad.

                              Hazrat Khadija was the wife of the Holy Prophet (P.B.U.H).

                              Hazrat Abu Talib was the uncle of the Holy Prophet (p.b.u.h)

                              Year of sorrow means غم کا سال

                              389

                              Name the first non-Arab Muslim who accepted Islam first?

                              Correct Answer: Hazrat Farwah Bin Umro Al Jazami RA
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              Hazrat Suwaid Bin Samit (R.A) was the first person from to Madina to accet Islam.

                               

                              390

                              How many letters in first Kalima?

                              Correct Answer: 26
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              Seven Words are there in the First Kalimah.

                              26 letters in first Kalima.

                              ’لاَ اِلٰہَ اِلَّا اﷲُ مُحَمَّدٌ رَّسُوْلُ اﷲ


                              Additonal information:

                              List of six Kalimas

                              1

                              كَلِمَاتْ اَلطَّيِّبَة

                              Kalimat aṭ-Ṭayyibah

                              (Word of Purity)

                              2

                              كَلِمَاتْ اَلشَّهَادَة‎

                              Kalimat ash-Shahādah

                              (Word of Testimony)

                              3

                              كَلِمَاتْ اَلتَّمْجِيدْ‎

                              Kalimat at-Tamjīd

                              (Word of Majesty)

                              4

                              كَلِمَاتْ اَلتَّوْحِيدْ‎

                              Kalimat at-Tawḥīd

                              (Word of Oneness)

                              5

                              كَلِمَاتْ إِسْتِغْفَارْ‎

                              Kalimat ʾIstighfār

                              (Word of Penitence)

                              6

                              كَلِمَاتْ رَدّْ اَلْكُفْرْ‎

                              Kalimat Radd al-Kufr

                              ("Word of Rejection of Disbelief")




                              391

                              Murtad is one who change his __________

                              Correct Answer: religion
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              Murtad is one who change his religion.

                              A person who leaves Islam after accepting it called Murtad۔

                              Abaid Ullah Bin Jaish was the first Murtad (Apostasy) in the history of Islam.

                              392

                              A minimum number of people required for Friday prayers ?

                              Correct Answer: 3
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation
                               A minimum three number of people is required for Friday prayers.
                              The presence of females or minors only is not enough.
                              مصر یا فنائے مصر (اطراف شہر ) یا بڑی بستی میں امام کے علاوہ کم ازکم تین عاقل بالغ افراد موجود ہوں تو   نمازِ جمعہ کی ادائیگی درست ہے۔ جمعے کی جماعت کے لیے تین بالغ مرد مقتدیوں کا ہونا کافی ہے۔ یہی امام ابوحنیفہ رحمہ اللہ کا قول ہے۔ (کفایت المفتی 3/254دارالاشاعت)
                              Additional Information
                              Hazrat Usman RA was the Companion of the Holy Prophet SAW who added/started the second Azan in Namaz-e-Jumma
                              The first Jummah prayer offered in Madinah
                              Source Click here
                              393

                              How many parts of each Hadis are there____?

                              Correct Answer: Two
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              There are the two parts of each of the Hadis: Sanad and Matan

                              1. Sanad (Chain) (Names of Transmitters)

                              2. Matan (Words of Hadith

                              394

                              How was the House of Allah disappeared?

                              Correct Answer: Flood
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              the House of Allah disappeared by Flood.

                              395

                              Which is first Madni Surah in Quran?

                              Correct Answer: Al-Baqarah
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation
                              The first complete Madani surah of the Quran is Al-Baqarah
                              Al-Baqarah is the second and longest chapter of the Quran.
                              It consists of 286 verses, 6,201 words, and 25,500 letters.
                              The total number of Surah in the Holy Quran is 114.
                              1. Makki Surah is 86 
                              2. Madni is 28
                              396

                              What is the penalty for the thief?

                              Correct Answer: Clipping of Hands
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation
                              • In Islamic law (Sharia), the penalty for theft (Sariqa) is amputation of the hand, specifically the right hand, as mentioned in the Quran (Surah Al-Ma'idah, verse 38).
                              • This punishment is applied in cases where the theft is proven and meets certain conditions, and is intended to serve as a deterrent and a means of rehabilitation.
                              397

                              Deen e Hanif is the old name of____?

                              Correct Answer: Islam
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              The history of Islam concerns the political, social, economic and cultural developments of Islamic civilization. Most historians accept that Islam originated in Mecca and Medina at the start of the 7th century CE.

                              398

                              What was the title of Hazrat Ayesha (رضہ)?

                              Correct Answer: Siddiqa
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              Hazrat Ayesha (رضہ) was the daughter of Hazrat Abu Baker & Wife Of Hazrat Muhammad (PBUH)

                              Aisha was married to Muhammad in Mecca in 624 CE, after the Battle of Badr.
                              Resting                               place Jannat al-Baqi, Medina

                              Hazrat Ayesha Remained alive 46 years after the demise of Muhammad (PBUH).

                              Siddiqa was the title of Hazrat Ayesha (رضہ) meaning 'the truthful woman, daughter of the truthful man

                              Hazrat Ayesha is also known for narrating 2,210 hadiths


                              "Hazrat Muhammad PBUH Had 11th Wives.

                              1.       HAZRAT KHADEJA,

                              2.       HAZRAT SAUDA

                              3.       HAZRAT AYESHA

                              4.       HAZRAT HAFSA

                              5.       HAZRAT ZAINAB KHUZEMA

                              6.       HAZRAT UMM E SALMA

                              7.       HAZRAT ZAINAB BINT E JAHESH

                              8.       HAZRAT JAVERIA

                              9.       HAZRAT UMM E HABIBA

                              10.   HAZRAT SAFIA

                              11.   HAZRAT MEMONA RA 

                              ND29-5-2023


                              399

                              Which prophet died while standing with the support of a stick?

                              Correct Answer: Hazrat Suleman A.S
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation
                              Prophet Sulaiman عَلَيْهِ ٱلسَّلَامُ died  in 931 B.C.
                              Hazrat Suleman A.S died while standing with the support of a stick
                              the title of Hazrat Suleman AS is King of Israel
                              Hazrat Suleman knew the language of birds, to both animals and jinn
                              The Mausoleum of Prophet Sulaiman (PBUH) can be found within the compound of Masjid-Al-Aqsa

                              400

                              Hajj was ordered in_____?

                              Correct Answer: Sura e Baqara
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                               * Hajj means to intend.

                              • Hajj made compulsory in 9 A.H.

                              • First Hajj offered in 9 A.H.

                              • Hajj ordained in Surah Bakr.

                              • The holy prophet performed only 1 Hajj in 10th A.H.

                              • There are 3 types of Hajj.

                              • One tawaf of Kaaba is known as a Shoot.

                              • Tawaf begins from Shoot.

                              • Number of Jamarat is 3.

                              • Mosque located in Mina is Kheef.

                              • At Meekat, Hujjaj assumes the state of Ihram.

                              • Kalima Tauheed is recited during Hajj.

                              • At Mina the ritual of offering sacrifice is performed


                              401

                              Which Ghazawa is mentioned in Surah Taubah?

                              Correct Answer: Tabuk
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation
                              • The Ghazawa of Tabuk is mentioned in Surah At-Tawbah, which is the ninth chapter of the Quran.
                              • The Ghazawa of Tabuk took place in October 630 CE and was the last military expedition led by the Prophet Muhammad (SAW).
                              402

                              It was Will of Allah that Hazrat Muhammad PBUH married to?

                              Correct Answer: Hazrat Zainab Bint Jahsh RA
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              It was Will of Allah that Hazrat Muhammad PBUH married to Hazrat Zainab Bint Jahsh RA.

                              403

                              Duration of revelation of Holy Quran is?

                              Correct Answer: 22 years 02 months 22 days
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              The duration of the revelation of the Holy Quran is  22 years, 02 months, and 22 days, between 610 to 632 AD.

                              404

                              How many Hufaaz were martyred in the battle against Musailimah Kazab, the false prophet-hood claimant?

                              Correct Answer: 70
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              Seventy (70) Hufaaz were martyred in the battle against Musailimah Kazab, the false prophet-hood claimant.

                              405

                              What was the real name of Mujjaddid-e-Alf Sani?

                              Correct Answer: Shaikh Ahmed Sirhindi
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation
                              • The real name of Mujaddid-e-Alf Sani was Ahmad.
                              • Ahmad Sirhindi, also known as Mujaddid-i-Alf-i-Thani, was an Indian Islamic scholar, a Hanafi jurist and a member of the Naqshbandī Sufi order.
                              • He was born in 1564 and died in 1624.
                              •  He was a prominent figure in the Mughal Empire and played a significant role in promoting Islamic orthodoxy.
                              406

                              In which year Hajr-e-Aswad was stolen?

                              Correct Answer: 930 CE
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation
                              • The Black Stone was stolen in 930 CE by the Qarmatians, who held it for ransom and returned it 23 years later.
                              • The Black Stone is a rock set into the eastern corner of the Kaaba, the ancient building in the center of the Grand Mosque in Mecca, Saudi Arabia.
                              407

                              First Takbir (Allah Akbar) in Prayers is called?

                              Correct Answer: Takbirah al Ihram
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation
                              • Takbirah al Ihram, also known as the "First Takbir", is the initial declaration of "Allahu Akbar" (الله أكبر) that marks the beginning of the Islamic prayer (Salah).
                              • It is called Takbirah al Ihram because it is the threshold between the state of not praying and entering into the prayer.
                              408

                              When the Ghazwa of Badr Al-Sughra took place?

                              Correct Answer: 4 Α.Η
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              Ghazwa-e-Sughra, refers to the Battle of Badr al-Sughra, which took place in 4 AH (626 CE).

                              409

                              What are the three "White Days" in Islam?

                              Correct Answer: 13th 14th and 15th days of every month in the Islamic calendar
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation
                              • The "White Days" (Ayyam al-Beedh) refer to the 13th, 14th, and 15th days of every month in the Islamic calendar, which are considered special days for fasting, prayer, and charity.
                              • These days are considered "white" because the moon is full and bright during this time.
                              410

                              Who made the silver encasement of Hajr e Aswad for the first time?

                              Correct Answer: Hazrat Abdullah bin Zubair (RA)
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation
                              • The silver encasement of Hajr e Aswad was made for the first time by Abdullah bin Zubair (RA).
                              • Abdullah bin Zubair (RA) was the first person to put a silver container around the Hajr e Aswad to protect it from any future attacks, as it had been damaged by catapults during the Umayyad siege of Mecca in 683 CE.
                              411

                              How many salaah will the Takbeer-e-Tashreeq be recited?

                              Correct Answer: 23 Salaah
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation
                              • The Takbeer-e-Tashreeq will be recited after 23 Salaah.
                              • The Takbeer-e-Tashreeq is a declaration of faith recited by Muslims during the days of Hajj and Eid Al-Adha.
                              • It is recited after every Fardh Salah, starting on the 9th of Dhul Hijjah, the day of Arafah, and continues until the Asr prayer on the 13th of Dhul Hijjah.
                              412

                              To whom did the Holy Prophet Hazrat Muhammad (PBUH) give the good news of wearing the bracelet of Kasra?

                              Correct Answer: Hazrat Saraqa bin Jaishm bin Malik (RA)
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              To Hazrat Saraqa bin Jaishm bin Malik (RA) did the Holy Prophet Hazrat Muhammad (PBUH) give the good news of wearing the bracelet of Kasra.

                              413

                              During the Jahiliyyah (pre-Islamic era), which tribe held the post of embassy in Makkah?

                              Correct Answer: Banu Adi
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              During the Jahiliyyah (pre-Islamic era), the tribe that held the post of embassy in Makkah was Banu Adi.

                              414

                              What is the name of the person who shows the way in migration?

                              Correct Answer: Hazrat Abdullah bin Ariqat (RA)
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              The person who shows the way in migration is Hazrat Abdullah bin Ariqat (RA).

                              415

                              Who is the author of "Al-Adab al-Mufrad"?

                              Correct Answer: Imam Bukhari
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              The author of "Al-Adab al-Mufrad" is Imam Bukhari.

                              416

                              Which Prophet's community faced calamity due to cheating in measurement and weightage of things?

                              Correct Answer: Prophet Shoaib (AS)
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              The Prophet's community that faced calamity due to cheating in measurement and weightage of things is Prophet Shoaib's (AS) community.

                              417

                              What is meant by "Ayam Arab"?

                              Correct Answer: Arab Civil Wars
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              "Arab Civil Wars" is meant by "Ayam Arab."

                              418

                              According to Shah Wali Allah, how many verses in the Quran are considered abrogated?

                              Correct Answer: 5 verses
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              According to Shah Wali Allah, five verses in the Quran are considered abrogated.

                              419

                              What is a hadith that is directly narrated by a Tabi called?

                              Correct Answer: Maqtoo
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              A hadith that is directly narrated by a Tabi is called "Maqtoo."

                              420

                              What is the name of the campaign known as "Jaish-ul-Asra"?

                              Correct Answer: Battle of Tabuk
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation
                              • The campaign known as "Jaish-ul-Asra" (Army of Difficulty) refers to the Battle of Tabuk, which took place in 9 AH (630 CE).
                              • This battle was the last military campaign led by the Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) and was fought against the Byzantine Empire.
                              421

                              "Al-Hawi" is written by whom?

                              Correct Answer: Abu Bakr Zakaria Razi
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation
                              • "Al-Hawi" or "The Comprehensive Book on Medicine" is a medical book written by Abu Bakr Zakaria Razi, also known as Rhazes or Rasis, in the 10th century.
                              • The book is divided into 23 volumes, each covering specific parts or diseases of the body.
                              422

                              The punishments of locusts, frogs, and storms were revealed to which nation?

                              Correct Answer: The people of Hazrat Musa (AS) and Bani Israel
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              The punishments of locusts, frogs, and storms were revealed to the people of Hazrat Musa (AS) and Bani Israel.

                              423

                              Sam, Ham, and Yapheth were sons of which prophet?

                              Correct Answer: Hazrat Noah (AS)
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              Sam, Ham, and Yapheth were sons of Hazrat Noah (AS).

                              424

                              Who built Darulandah?

                              Correct Answer: Qasi bin Kalab
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              Darulandah was built by Qasi bin Kalab.

                              425

                              Who was the first to breastfeed the Holy Prophet (PBUH) among the maidservants?

                              Correct Answer: Hazrat Sobia (RA)
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              Hazrat Sobia (RA) was the first maidservant to breastfeed the Holy Prophet (PBUH) after his birth.

                              426

                              Who is the author of "The Declaration of Signatories"?

                              Correct Answer: Imam Ibn Qayyim Al-Jawziyya
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation
                              • "The Declaration of Signatories" (Al-'Aqidah Al-Waasitiyah) is a famous book written by Imam Ibn Qayyim Al-Jawziyya (1292-1350 CE), a renowned Islamic scholar and theologian.
                              • The book is a concise and authoritative exposition of Islamic creed and doctrine.
                              427

                              On the occasion of Hajj in 9 Hijri, which companion was sent to Makkah by the Holy Prophet (PBUH) as a messenger?

                              Correct Answer: Hazrat Ali ibn Abi Talib (RA)
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              On the occasion of Hajj in 9 Hijri, Hazrat Ali ibn Abi Talib (RA) was sent to Makkah by the Holy Prophet (PBUH) as a messenger.

                              428

                              Whose testimony was considered equal to that of two men among the companions?

                              Correct Answer: Hazrat Khuzaima ibn Thabit (RA)
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              Hazrat Khuzaima ibn Thabit (RA) had his testimony considered equal to that of two men.

                              429

                              How many copies of the Quran did Shah Wali Allah write?

                              Correct Answer: 3
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation
                              • Shah Wali Allah wrote three handwritten copies of the Quran, which are considered priceless treasures of Islamic calligraphy and scholarship.
                              • One copy is preserved in the National Archives of India, while the other two are in private collections.
                              430

                              Ghazwa Banu Qurayzah was fought immediately after which battle?

                              Correct Answer: Battle of Khandaq (Trench)
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              Ghazwa Banu Qurayzah was fought immediately after the Battle of Khandaq (Trench).

                              431

                              In which Surah of the Holy Quran are women commanded to cover themselves?

                              Correct Answer: Surah An-Nur
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              Women are commanded to cover themselves in Surah An-Nur, verse 31.

                              432

                              Who did the Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) send a letter to on the occasion of Hajj in 9 Hijri?

                              Correct Answer: The rulers of neighboring countries
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              In 9 Hijri, the Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) sent letters to the rulers of neighboring countries, including the Roman Emperor Heraclius, the Persian Emperor Khosrau, and others, inviting them to accept Islam and warning them of the consequences of rejecting the truth.

                              433

                              Who was given the title Zaat un Nataqin?

                              Correct Answer: Hazrat Asma (RA)
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              Hazrat Asma (RA) was given the title Zaat un Nataqin.

                              Which means "She of the Two Belts"

                              Her nickname was given to her by Muhammad during the migration to Medina

                              She prepared food for this journey.

                              She tied the food on the camel's back with her belt, as nothing else could be found


                              She was Abu Bakr's daughter.

                              Her mother was Qutaylah bint Abd al-Uzza

                              She was half-sisters were Aisha and Umm Kulthum bint Abi Bakr,

                              434

                              In which Surah is the mention of Hajj, Fasting etc?

                              Correct Answer: Surah Al-Baqarah
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              The mention of Hajj, fasting, and other religious obligations is found in: Surah Al-Baqarah.

                              435

                              When did Hazrat Imam Tirmazy die?

                              Correct Answer: October 9, 892 CE
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              Muhammad ibn Isa al-Tirmidhi, often referred to as Imām at-Termezī or Tirmidhī, was an Islamic scholar who died on October 9, 892 CE.

                              436

                              How many words exist in Quran Majeed

                              Correct Answer: 77432
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              The Quran Majeed contains approximately 77,432 words.

                              The number of verses is generally agreed to be 6,236, excluding the Bismillah, or 6,348

                              437

                              Before invading Sindh, bin Qasim was the governor of?

                              Correct Answer: Fars
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              Before invading Sindh, bin Qasim was the governor of Fars.

                              438

                              Who levied taxes on new converts to Islam?

                              Correct Answer: Umar ibn al-Khattab
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              Jizya tax was imposed on Non-Muslims during the Holy Prophet's (PBUH) life

                              But it was formalized during Caliph Umar bin Khatab.

                              439

                              Mualim Thani is also known as?

                              Correct Answer: Minhaj Siraj
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              Mualim Thani is also known as Minhaj Siraj.

                              440

                              When was the Battle of Nahrawan fought?

                              Correct Answer: July 17, 658 AD
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              The Battle of Nahrawan was fought on July 17, 658 AD (Safar 38 AH).

                              The Battle of Nahrawan was fought between Hazrat Ali (RA) and the Kharjis.

                              441

                              Who wrote the book "Taqat e Nasri"?

                              Correct Answer: Minhaj Siraj
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              "Taqat e Nasri" was written by Minhaj Siraj.

                              442

                              Which false prophet was killed during the life of Holy Prophet Muhammad (SAW)?

                              Correct Answer: Musailama
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              Musaylimah was a false prophet who claimed prophethood during the lifetime of HolyProphet Muhammad (SAW)

                              He was killed in the Battle of Yamama in 632 CE.

                              Musailama al Kazzab False Prophet Was Called The Liar’ By The Holy Prophet 

                              Wahshi ibn Harb killed Muslema Kazab (Musailamah al Kazzab)

                              443

                              What was the approximate year when the Umayyad rule in Spain ended?

                              Correct Answer: 1031 CE
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              The Umayyad rule in Spain ended in 1031 CE.

                              444

                              When did the Prophet Muhammad (SAW) perform Umrah after the Treaty of Hudaybiyah?

                              Correct Answer: The following year
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation
                              • The Treaty of Hudaybiyah was signed in 628 CE, and the Prophet Muhammad (SAW) performed Umrah in 629 CE, the following year.
                              • This was part of the agreement reached in the treaty, which allowed the Muslims to perform Umrah in Mecca.
                              445

                              Who was the Persian general in the Battle of al-Qādisiyyah?

                              Correct Answer: Rostam Farrokhzād
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              The Persian general in the Battle of al-Qādisiyyah was Rostam Farrokhzād.

                              446

                              What was the Expedition of Usra?

                              Correct Answer: A military expedition led by the Prophet Muhammad (SAW)
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              The Expedition of Usra, also known as the Expedition of Usra ibn al-Huqaq.


                              Here are some key points about the Expedition of Usra:

                              • Date: 6th Hijri year (627 CE)
                              • Leader: Prophet Muhammad (SAW)
                              • Purpose: To defend against a potential threat from the tribe of Usra
                              • Outcome: Successful defeat of the tribe
                              • Location: Usra (a place near Medina)
                              447

                              Which Surah was revealed after the Battle of Badr?

                              Correct Answer: Surah Al-Anfal
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              Surah Al-Anfal: This Surah was revealed after the Battle of Badr and contains verses that relate to the battle, including 8:5-19, 8:42-48, 8:70-71.

                              448
